Wider Sports News.title: Too many sports sponsorships are operating in a silo – here’s how to fix it
Wider Sports News.summary: Trunk's Adam Britton explains why the agency launched PartnerAmp and how the new offering will unlock better sponsorship results. The post Too many sports sponsorships are operating in a silo – here’s how to fix it appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
There is no shortage of ambition in sports partnerships. We see it when the deal is signed, in the rights package, in the launch moment, and in the excitement around what the partnership could become. But too often, that is where the clarity starts to fade.

Over the last 12 months, we have spent a lot of time speaking to brands, clubs and rights holders about how partnerships are really working in practice. The same theme kept coming back. The issue was rarely the partnership itself. More often, it was what happened after the deal was done.

Wider Sports News.title: Why more training alone won’t advance women leaders
Wider Sports News.summary: When most organisations think about developing female leaders, they focus on skills, workshops and courses. But new research suggests the greatest value of women's leadership initiatives may have little to do with training at all. The post Why more training alone won’t advance women leaders appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
Over the past decade, women’s leadership programmes have become one of the sport industry’s most visible responses to the underrepresentation of women in leadership. The logic seems straightforward. If women are not advancing into senior roles at the same rate as men, perhaps they need more training, more confidence, or more leadership skills.

But where does this assumption come from? It reflects a long-standing tendency to locate the problem in women rather than in the systems shaping career progression. If women are underrepresented in leadership, the assumption is that they need more development to compete. Many leadership programmes have been built on this premise.

Wider Sports News.title: ‘The most valuable real estate’: How the 2026 World Cup became a springboard for AI innovation
Wider Sports News.summary: Four years on from the launch of ChatGPT, the 2026 World Cup is the first edition of sport’s biggest event to truly integrate AI across the board, with Lenovo playing a major role. With intelligent technologies influencing everything from tournament broadcasts to referee systems, SportsPro breaks down how AI is transforming how the competition is operated and consumed by fans. The post ‘The most valuable real estate’: How the 2026 World Cup became a springboard for AI innovation appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
If the four-year gap between Fifa World Cups is a long time in soccer-related terms, the cycle represents an absolute eon when it comes to technology. The internet was a niche curiosity at France 98, but by the time the tournament arrived in Japan and South Korea in 2002 it was a vital source of engagement. Subsequent quadrennial advances were driven by the mobile phone, smartphone and faster connectivity, all of which powered live streaming capabilities and a wide array of digital services that transformed how fans interacted with the world’s biggest sporting event.

At Brazil 2014 and Russia 2018, developments spilled onto the pitch, with Video Assistant Referees (VAR), Goal-line technology (GLT) and electronic performance and tracking systems (EPTS) transforming how the game was played.

Wider Sports News.body:

Wider Sports News.title: Serena Williams plans return to Wimbledon for singles competition
Wider Sports News.summary: Serena Williams plans return to Wimbledon for singles competition NBC News
Wider Sports News.body:
Serena Williams will return to Wimbledon for the 2026 singles competition as a wild card, the tournament announced Sunday. The tennis legend and 23-time Grand Slam singles champion made her return to professional tennis earlier this month at the HSBC Championships in London, where she competed in the doubles event. That run ended prematurely after an injury to her doubles partner.

Last week, it was announced Williams and her sister Venus would play doubles together at Wimbledon — more than two decades after they won their first title together there. It marks a major comeback for the 44-year-old superstar, who announced in 2022 she was “evolving away from tennis” and had not competed in nearly four years.

Wider Sports News.title: “I would never imagine lifting this trophy” – Francisco Cerundolo on historic HSBC Championships title
Wider Sports News.summary: “I would never imagine lifting this trophy” – Francisco Cerundolo on historic HSBC Championships title LTA
Wider Sports News.body:
The biggest title of his career, the first Queen’s Club champion from Argentina, and getting to celebrate with his family – it was a day to remember for Francisco Cerundolo at the HSBC Championships.

Cerundolo fought back from a set and a break down against former champion Tommy Paul to beat the American 6-7(5), 6-4, 6-3 in what was the longest final in Queen’s Club history.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC Championships 2026: Marcelo Arevalo & Mate Pavic seal men’s doubles title
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC Championships 2026: Marcelo Arevalo & Mate Pavic seal men’s doubles title LTA
Wider Sports News.body:
Marcelo Arevalo and Mate Pavic were crowned champions at the HSBC Championships after beating Britain's Henry Patten and Harri Heliovaara in the final. The fourth seeds upset the world No.1 duo 6-2, 6-4 to win their first Queen’s Club trophy as a team.

Pavic – who also won the title in 2022 – now has the most ATP doubles titles of any active player with 43, nine of which have come on grass. They defeated three pairs with British players this week, including Joe Salisbury and Rajeev Ram in the first round, and Julian Cash and Lloyd Glasspool in the semi-finals.

Wider Sports News.title: “Super proud”: Cerundolo becomes the first Argentinian to win Queen’s, fighting back to deny Paul
Wider Sports News.summary: “Super proud”: Cerundolo becomes the first Argentinian to win Queen’s, fighting back to deny Paul tennismajors.com
Wider Sports News.body:
A set and a break down, then the first Argentinian champion in Queen’s history: Francisco Cerundolo (No 7) beat Tommy Paul 6-7 (4), 6-4, 6-3 to take the title, ending Paul’s nine-match streak at the event and winning his fifth career trophy.

Argentinian seventh seed Francisco Cerundolo recovered from a set and a break down to beat American Tommy Paul 6-7 (4), 6-4, 6-3 on Sunday afternoon, becoming the first man from his country to win the HSBC Championships and the new grass-court champion at Queen’s Club.

Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC Championships 2026: Francisco Cerundolo battles back to win Queen’s Club title LTA
Wider Sports News.body:
Francisco Cerundolo came back from a set and a break down to defeat former champion Tommy Paul and lift the biggest title of his career at the HSBC Championships. The seventh seed became the first Argentinian player to win The Queen’s Club title with a 6-7(4), 6-4, 6-2 victory over Paul in the final.

Cerundolo fought for over three hours in 30-degree heat to claim his fifth career title and his first at an ATP 500 level. The world No.27 missed three match points on the Paul serve at 5-2 in the third set before holding his nerve to see out an historic victory. It was the longest final in Queen’s Club history after Marin Cilic vs Novak Djokovic in 2018.

Wider Sports News.title: Where to watch Tommy Paul in HSBC Championships tennis final today on free streams, TV
Wider Sports News.summary: Where to watch Tommy Paul in HSBC Championships tennis final today on free streams, TV PennLive.com
Wider Sports News.body:
American Tommy Paul competes for the men’s singles title at the HSBC Championships with a Sunday final against Argentina’s Francisco Cerundolo. The match is scheduled to start at 8:30 a.m. ET with TV coverage on T2 and streaming on-demand.

- How to watch: Live streams of the Paul vs. Cerundolo match are available with offers from FuboTV (free trial), DirecTV (free trial) and SlingTV (low intro rate). Paul is in the men’s final in his first action since falling to No. 15 Casper Ruud in the French Open, reaching this stage with a run of four straight-sets victories. He defeated No. 4 seed Alejandro Davidovich Fokina (6-3, 7-6) in the quarterfinals and Ugo Humbert (6-3, 6-3) in the semis to reach his fourth tournament final of the season. Paul went 1-2 in his previous three finals, beating Roman Andres Burruchaga in the U.S Men’s Clay Court Championship in March and falling to Ignacio Buse at the Hamburg Open and Sebastian Korda at the Delray Beach Open.

Wider Sports News.title: What Tfl disruptions will affect HSBC Championships at The Queen’s Club?
Wider Sports News.summary: What Tfl disruptions will affect HSBC Championships at The Queen’s Club? LTA
Wider Sports News.body:
What Tfl disruptions will affect HSBC Championships at The Queen’s Club? Spectators attending the HSBC Championships should be aware of a number of planned transport disruptions that may affect their travel plans to the tournament. This includes planned engineering work by TfL and the threat of industrial action by the RMT union.

The LTA has requested TfL re-schedule the engineering work to minimise disruption to spectators, but TfL have not been able to do this, and we are now working to provide guidance and alternative routes for those travelling to The Queen’s Club.

Wider Sports News.title: ATP Final: Francisco Cerundolo vs Tommy Paul Live Stream: How To Watch 2026 HSBC Championships Tennis
Wider Sports News.summary: ATP Final: Francisco Cerundolo vs Tommy Paul Live Stream: How To Watch 2026 HSBC Championships Tennis Fubo
Wider Sports News.body:
Tommy Paul takes on Francisco Cerundolo in the 2026 HSBC Championships and his second title of the year. The ATP Finals of the 2026 HSBC Championships is down to eighth-seed Tommy Paul and seventh-seed Francisco Cerundolo. This is the fourth final of 2026 for Paul, who is in search of his second title of the year. Paul’s journey to the finals saw the 21st-ranked player in the world run through the competition without dropping a set. He needed two tiebreakers to do so, but otherwise Paul is playing some of the best tennis of his career in 2026. Cerundolo has needed all three sets in three of his four matches, including three tiebreakers to reach his second final of the year.

Live stream Francisco Cerundolo vs Tommy Paul on Fubo: Start watching now! This is the eighth career head-to-head match between Paul and Cerundolo. The edge through seven matches goes to Cerundolo, who has won five matches against Paul. Cerundolo has won three matches in a row against Paul, including the only head-to-head last season.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC Championships 2026: Francisco Cerundolo reaches biggest final of his career at Queen’s
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC Championships 2026: Francisco Cerundolo reaches biggest final of his career at Queen’s LTA
Wider Sports News.body:
Francisco Cerundolo will play the biggest final of his career on Sunday, with the Argentine now just one match away from the HSBC Championships title. Cerundolo had to come through yet another three-set epic in his semi-final against Brandon Nakashima to win 6-7(5), 6-3, 6-4.

It was a marathon battle lasting two hours and 43 minutes, but the Argentinian star just had that extra quality in the big moments to see himself over the line. “I’m super happy to be honest,” he said. “This was a really challenging match. I think Brandon is playing incredible, amazing during the whole week and today also.

Wider Sports News.summary: Semifinals: Nakashima vs Cerundolo and Paul vs Humbert Live Stream: How To Watch 2026 HSBC Championships Fubo
Wider Sports News.body:
Brandon Nakashima, Francisco Cerundolo, Tommy Paul, and Ugo Humbert all look to advance in the semifinals of the 2026 HSBC Championships. The semifinals are set in the 2026 HSBC Championships. After upsetting top-seeded Alex De Minaur in the quarterfinals, Brandon Nakashima takes on seventh-seeded Francisco Cerundolo. In the quarterfinals, Cerundolo survived a three-set match against local favorite, unseeded Arthur Frey. The second semifinal features eighth-seed Tommy Paul looking to make his fifth singles final of 2026 against Ugo Humbert. The championship match will take place on Sunday between the two semifinal winners to crown the 2026 HSBC Championship. This is the first career match between Nakashima and Cerundolo, who are each looking to add to their mantle with another ATP title.

Wider Sports News.title: Apple streams Austrian GP for free in bid to expand US F1 audience
Wider Sports News.summary: Follows deal with rival streaming giant Netflix to simulcast Canadian Grand Prix. The post Apple streams Austrian GP for free in bid to expand US F1 audience appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
Apple will make the Formula One Austrian Grand Prix free to access for anyone who downloads Apple TV and has an Apple ID in the US. Coverage will include the entire race weekend – practice, qualifying and the Grand Prix – for Formula One’s visit to the Red Bull Ring from 26th to 28th June.

The first and third practice sessions start in the early hours of the morning, but the race itself gets underway at a palatable 6am (PT) or 9am (ET) on the Sunday. This forms part of continued efforts by Formula One and Apple to open up the sport to new viewers after the streaming platform acquired the rights from the start of this season for a reported US$150 million per year.

Wider Sports News.title: ATP fines tennis player Corentin Moutet $40,000 for profane interview following HSBC Championships win
Wider Sports News.summary: ATP fines tennis player Corentin Moutet $40,000 for profane interview following HSBC Championships win Fox News
Wider Sports News.body:
Tennis fans may have found the sport's next "bad boy," but an R-rated on-court interview may have that outlaw's wallet feeling a little lighter. Following a win at the HSBC Championships on Monday, French tennis player Corentin Moutet was interviewed at center court and unleashed a flurry of F-bombs into the reporter's microphone.

It was obviously meant as a joke, but several fans and officials didn't treat it as a laughing matter, including the ATP. In an official statement sent to OutKick Friday morning, the ATP communications office revealed that Moutet is being fined $40,000 for his profane outburst on Monday.

Wider Sports News.title: Trunk launches PartnerAmp to help brands and rights holders make sports partnerships work harder
Wider Sports News.summary: New offer combines 12 months of research with a free Partnership Health Score designed to help teams identify where partnerships are underpowered and where stronger thinking could unlock better results. The post Trunk launches PartnerAmp to help brands and rights holders make sports partnerships work harder appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
Trunk has launched PartnerAmp, a new strategic offer designed to help brands, clubs and rights holders build stronger sports partnerships by connecting activation to wider business goals, marketing priorities and season-long strategy.

The launch follows 12 months of research and development, shaped by conversations with clubs, rights holders and brands, which highlighted a clear gap in the market. While many sports partnerships still rely heavily on visibility, there is often less clarity around how they connect back to the wider business, how they support the marketing plan, and how they create more meaningful long-term value.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC expands Google Cloud AI partnership with $100 million use case threshold
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC expands Google Cloud AI partnership with $100 million use case threshold The Asian Banker
Wider Sports News.body:
HSBC and Google Cloud announced an expanded multi-year AI partnership on 17 June 2026, extending a relationship that now spans more than 600 of the bank's applications running on Google's infrastructure. The agreement gives HSBC access to Gemini models and the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, with forward-deployed engineering teams from Google Cloud and Google DeepMind working directly alongside HSBC staff.

The programme covers three initial delivery areas: hyper-personalised wealth management support, financial crime risk management and AI tools for frontline staff and relationship managers. HSBC expects it to enable more than 200 new AI use cases over two years.

Wider Sports News.title: Corentin Moutet handed steep fine for foul-mouthed outburst in HSBC Championship at Queen's Club
Wider Sports News.summary: Corentin Moutet handed steep fine for foul-mouthed outburst in HSBC Championship at Queen's Club Sky Sports
Wider Sports News.body:
Corentin Moutet has been fined almost all of his prize money at Queen's Club following his foul-mouthed post-match interview. The controversial Frenchman said the 'F-word' seven times live on BBC television after his first-round victory over Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard.

Having initially sworn unintentionally while answering the first question, a grinning Moutet then responded by saying the profanity three times more, before interviewer Jenny Drummond pulled the plug.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC and Google Cloud partner to develop AI tools
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC and Google Cloud partner to develop AI tools National Technology News
Wider Sports News.body:
HSBC has announced a multi-year deal with Google Cloud to build and deploy AI tools across its global operations. The bank said the partnership will accelerate its shift towards AI-enabled ways of working in areas including hyper-personalised advice and financial crime risk management.

The partnership gives HSBC access to Google’s latest agentic AI capabilities, including its Gemini models and Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. HSBC said the move will provide it with more than 200 new AI use cases over the next two years, simplifying processes as well as helping it to identify and prioritise the highest value initiatives for investment and delivery. The bank estimates that this could return more than $100 million in direct revenue gains and wider efficiency improvements.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC Targets 200 AI Use Cases in Google Cloud Partnership
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC Targets 200 AI Use Cases in Google Cloud Partnership digit.fyi
Wider Sports News.body:
HSBC and Google Cloud have announced a multi-year strategic partnership to build and deploy AI capabilities across the bank’s global operations, with an initial focus on wealth management, financial crime risk management and AI tools for frontline staff.

The partnership, announced at Google Cloud Summit London, is expected to support more than 200 new AI use cases at HSBC over the next two years. The bank said the collaboration will help accelerate AI adoption across its products and services globally, while prioritising high-value initiatives where HSBC estimates the benefit could exceed $100 million through direct revenue gains or wider efficiency improvements.

Wider Sports News.title: How HSBC & Google Cloud are Scaling AI Across Global Banking
Wider Sports News.summary: How HSBC & Google Cloud are Scaling AI Across Global Banking FinTech Magazine
Wider Sports News.body:
HSBC is set to embed AI more deeply into its global operations. The UK-based banking group has entered a strategic partnership with Google Cloud to roll out AI capabilities across its international business, with an initial emphasis on three core areas: personalised wealth management services, financial crime risk management and AI-driven decision support for frontline employees.

Under the multi-year agreement, teams from both organisations will collaborate to develop advanced AI tools aimed at improving the personalisation of HSBC’s operations. The bank anticipates that the initiative will enable more than 200 new AI use cases within the next two years.

Wider Sports News.body:
A newly announced, multi-year partnership between HSBC and Google Cloud will enable the financial institution to work with engineering teams from Google Cloud and DeepMind to develop new AI-powered tools and capabilities. The partnership will allow HSBC to benefit from access to Google’s latest agentic AI capabilities including Gemini and the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

The agreement will enable more than 200 new AI use cases for HSBC over the next two years, with a focus on the highest value initiatives for investment and delivery. HSBC estimates each of these could return more than $100 million in either direct revenue gains or broader efficiency improvements.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC and Google Cloud forge multi-year AI partnership
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC and Google Cloud forge multi-year AI partnership FinTech Global
Wider Sports News.body:
HSBC, one of the world’s largest banking and financial services organisations, has struck a multi-year strategic partnership with Google Cloud to roll out artificial intelligence capabilities across its global operations, with the bank estimating that its highest-priority initiatives could each deliver more than $100m in value.

The collaboration is expected to unlock more than 200 new AI use cases at HSBC within the next two years. The bank will gain access to Google’s latest agentic AI technology, including Gemini models and the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, and will work directly alongside Google Cloud and Google DeepMind engineering teams. HSBC already has more than 600 applications running on Google Cloud infrastructure.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC Championships 2026: Wild card Arthur Fery impresses in second round win over Adrian Mannarino
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC Championships 2026: Wild card Arthur Fery impresses in second round win over Adrian Mannarino LTA
Wider Sports News.body:
Arthur Fery’s Queen’s Club dream continues as the 23-year-old British star booked a place in his first ATP quarter-final at the HSBC Championships. Fery relished his debut on the Andy Murray Arena – putting in an impressive performance to knock out the experienced Adrian Mannarino 7-6(7), 6-4 in an hour and 42 minutes.

He becomes only the seventh British wild card to reach the last eight at Queen’s, joining Chris Bailey (1989), James Ward (2011), Kyle Edmund (2016), Jack Draper (2021), Ryan Peniston (2022), and Billy Harris (2024).

Wider Sports News.title: Queen's tennis: Britain's Arthur Fery reaches HSBC Championships quarter-finals
Wider Sports News.summary: Queen's tennis: Britain's Arthur Fery reaches HSBC Championships quarter-finals BBC
Wider Sports News.body:
British number five Arthur Fery recorded the "best result of my career" as he beat Adrian Mannarino to keep home hopes alive in the HSBC Championships at Queen's. The world number 140 reached his first ATP Tour quarter-final with a 7-6 (9-7) 6-4 victory over the veteran Frenchman.

The 23-year-old wildcard needed treatment for a nosebleed at the start of the second set but kept his focus to wrap up victory on Andy Murray Arena. Fery was ranked 461 when he upset 20th seed Alexei Popyrin on the opening day of last year's Wimbledon, but said his win over Mannarino was better.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC and Google Cloud Announce Transformative AI Banking Partnership
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC and Google Cloud Announce Transformative AI Banking Partnership Fintech Finance
Wider Sports News.body:
WHY THIS MATTERS: The era of experimental artificial intelligence in banking is rapidly yielding to a phase of deep institutional integration. By committing to an expansive, multi-year deployment of advanced generative models, this collaboration signals a critical maturation point in digital transformation for the financial sector. We are witnessing a fundamental pivot where Tier-1 institutions are moving beyond superficial, customer-facing chatbots to hardwire agentic AI into the bedrock of internal operations—specifically financial crime prevention and wealth advisory services. For the broader industry, this move validates that the next competitive frontier isn’t just about adopting new tech, but about operationalizing complex machine learning architectures to unlock substantial, quantifiable value. Banks that fail to emulate this depth of integration risk falling behind a new standard of efficiency, as the capacity to synthesize real-time data for personalized, regulatory-compliant outcomes becomes the primary benchmark for institutional resilience and market agility.

HSBC and Google Cloud today announced a multi-year partnership to build and deploy AI capabilities across HSBC’s operations globally, accelerating innovation in areas such as hyper-personalised advice and financial crime risk management.

Wider Sports News.title: Bale targets Cardiff City with new sports fund

Rugby.title: Reds snag Tigers and Blues assistants to finalise Cotter's coaching ticket
Rugby.summary: The Queensland Reds have confirmed Vern Cotter's coaching ticket, with Peter Hewat and Craig McGrath joining the Kiwi at Ballymore.
Rugby.body:
The Queensland Reds have confirmed Vern Cotter’s coaching ticket, with Peter Hewat and Craig McGrath joining the Kiwi at Ballymore. The pair have signed two-year deals to join Cotter and Zane Hilton in the new Reds setup, with McGrath following the head coach across the Tasman from the Blues while Hewat joins from a two-year stint with Leicester Tigers.

A former Waratahs and London Irish flyer, Hewat returns to the state he called home during his schooling at Nudgee College, having tasted Super Rugby coaching with the Brumbies from 2018-2020, and previously coached a handful of Reds stars at the U18 representative level.

Rugby.title: Laetitia Royer column: 'I am ready to be fully myself in the PWR Final'
Rugby.summary: You can feel emotion creeping into people’s voice when they talk about the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final now.
Rugby.body:
You can feel emotion creeping into people’s voice when they talk about the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final now. They want to win it. They want to go for the W. We’re going to step up. It is just right for Saracens to win it.

There is a lot of emotion. Every leader wants to talk more. Every leader wants to say more things. Personally, I’ve had some small injuries this season and I have been mainly focused on managing all that. That has really been the story of my season. Before the Christmas break I had only made three appearances in PWR. Now I feel more asserted in the team. I won’t be finishing this season on a personal low.

Rugby.title: Wallabies' winless tour still burns as chance to 'rectify' arrives
Rugby.summary: Still smarting seven months on, the Wallabies are intent on wiping the slate clean and starting the year with a bang in a sellout blockbuster against Ireland in Sydney.
Rugby.body:
Still smarting seven months on, the Wallabies are intent on wiping the slate clean and starting the year with a bang in a sellout blockbuster against Ireland in Sydney. Former captain Allan Alaalatoa is among the Wallabies stalwarts who endured the first four-Test winless tour of Europe since 1959 with successive losses to England, Italy, Ireland and France last November.

Alaalatoa remembers the gloomy tour like it was yesterday and admits the motivation to atone on July 4 and set the Wallabies back on the path to World Cup contention on home soil next year is driving the squad.

Rugby.title: Selections aside, Dave Rennie is pushing the right buttons with All Blacks
Rugby.summary: Everyone wants winners and losers out of any team naming. Frankly, when the net’s cast this wide, those kinds of categories are redundant.
Rugby.body:
The All Blacks’ 1987 Rugby World Cup-winning squad consisted of 26 players. It’s hard not to think of that when you see an initial squad of 34 picked to play the first three Tests of this season and contemplate that 44 players will then tour South Africa.

Everyone wants winners and losers out of any team naming. Frankly, when the net’s cast this wide, those kinds of categories are redundant. Forty-five men played Test rugby for New Zealand in 2025. So, if you missed the 34 to play France, Italy and Ireland, there’s every chance you’ll be in the 44 that goes to South Africa.

Rugby.title: Fit-again Callender to lead Wales against Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Fit-again Alex Callender to lead from the front when Wales take on the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Alex Callender fully intends to make the most of a "bonus" fixture when leading Wales out against the Barbarians on Saturday. The influential back-row forward will captain Sean Lynn's side when they play the second fixture of the double-header at Allianz Stadium in Twickenham, taking to the field at 17:00 BST after the men's game at 14:00 BST.

Callender will feature for Wales for the first time since last year's World Cup after an ankle injury forced her to miss the Six Nations. "I put no pressure on myself to come back for a game," said Callender. "This would've been a bonus if fit, so I am looking forward to the weekend.

Rugby.title: Ardie Savea details what success will look like for him as All Blacks captain
Rugby.summary: An admittedly nervous yet excited Ardie Savea was unveiled as All Blacks captain this week, sharing what the recognition means to him and what he hopes to achieve in the role.
Rugby.body:
An admittedly nervous yet excited Ardie Savea was unveiled as All Blacks captain this week, sharing what the recognition means to him and what he hopes to achieve in the role.

The All Blacks centurion has served as vice-captain for the past few seasons and stepped in as captain on numerous occasions, but taking on the responsibility full-time is new. The fan-favourite to assume the role, Savea succeeds Scott Barrett as captain, with the Crusaders lock having led for two years under Scott Robertson.

Rugby.title: Moana Pasifika officially out of 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season
Rugby.summary: New Zealand Rugby has confirmed that Moana Pasifika will not compete in the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season.
Rugby.body:
New Zealand Rugby has confirmed that Moana Pasifika will not compete in the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season. The announcement comes after the governing body, which controls Moana Pasifika’s licence to participate in the competition, concluded its exploration of “every possibility for a long-term solution” but was left unconvinced that the options would satisfy its criteria.

Multiple parties were involved in the process, but even after NZR pushed back its initial May 15 deadline, the capital and business plan requirements remained unmet. NZR outlined the requirements as 1) A credible business plan demonstrating the capacity to generate commercial revenue to operate on a sustainable basis over the long term, and 2) Evidence of sufficient committed capital to meet establishment costs, support operations in the short-term, and provide resilience against financial risks.

Rugby.title: On form it's Ruben Love then daylight for the All Blacks
Rugby.summary: The All Blacks coaching staff have reiterated that selections were made on form but when it comes to the No.10s it's Ruben Love then daylight.
Rugby.body:
When it comes to the All Blacks crop of No.10s selected Monday it’s Ruben Love then daylight. Veterans Damian McKenzie and Beauden Barrett have also been selected, as expected, but neither can match Love on form.

McKenzie had a nightmare performance in the Super Rugby final, but even before that, the 31-year-old wasn’t producing at the type of plays that made him a sensation 10 years ago. He’s in a different stage of his career where he should be providing the steady hand, not the one orchestrating an implosion.

Rugby.title: Remaining 2026 Black Ferns schedule confirmed, with free exhibition game added
Rugby.summary: All fixtures for the Black Ferns' expanded international season have now been finalised, with a free-to-attend exhibition match added to the slate of home games in August.
Rugby.body:
All fixtures for the Black Ferns’ expanded international season have now been finalised, with a free-to-attend exhibition match added to the slate of home games in August. Having already taken out the 2026 Pacific Four Series with three bonus-point wins in April, the team will next take the field on August 14 against the Black Ferns XV.

The exhibition match will be held in Upper Hutt, pitting the country’s top talent against those snapping at their heels, and will be free for fans to attend. While the rest of the international fixtures will be streamed live on Sky Sport, the Black Ferns XV match will also be free to watch on the Black Ferns’ YouTube and blackferns.com.

Rugby.title: North prepares for one last dance against Wales
Rugby.summary: George North will finish his sparkling rugby career on Saturday when he plays for the Barbarians against his country Wales at the Allianz Stadium in Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
George North will have one more opportunity to grace the rugby field this weekend when he lines up for the Barbarians against Wales. For a man who scored 47 tries in 121 internationals for his country, it will take some reconciliation that his final professional game will be in the black and white Baa-Baas jersey against the nation he served with such distinction for 14 years.

If it will be challenging for the man himself, who announced in April he will retire at the end of this season, North says it has been a particular struggle trying to explain the situation to his two sons, Jac and Tomi.

Rugby.title: Cardiff sign Tonga's Paea from Moana Pasifika
Rugby.summary: Cardiff sign Tonga back-five forward Semisi Paea from Super Rugby Pacific side Moana Pasifika
Rugby.body:
Cardiff have signed Tonga back-five forward Semisi Paea from Super Rugby Pacific side Moana Pasifika as they prepare for the 2026-27 season. The 27-year-old can play blindside flanker or lock and the Blue and Blacks also note his versatility across the back row.

Paea said: "This is an exciting new chapter in my rugby journey and I am thrilled to be joining such a well-respected club as Cardiff." With similarly gifted Wales forward Christ Tshiunza having opted to move from Exeter to Sale Sharks instead of seeking a return to his home city, Paea's arrival plugs a gap for Cardiff.

Rugby.title: The verdict on every women's team after 2026 SVNS World Championship
Rugby.summary: RugbyPass takes a final look at the twelve teams that took part in the 2026 HSBC SVNS World Championship.
Rugby.body:
The 2025-26 HSBC SVNS World Championship has come to its end, with the twelve teams across the men’s and women’s tournaments breaking for the next few months before preparations begin for 2026-27.

The women’s tournament saw a thrilling contest, with the finals ultimately coming down to a two-way horse race between Australia and New Zealand. It took until the final match of the season to split the teams – but the rivalry overshadowed a year where there were plenty of other great stories coming out of the women’s series.

Rugby.title: Borthwick’s ‘Burgess call’; Pollock to start? Chessum at six? Caluori unleashed? Five key issues from England squad
Rugby.summary: Can Janse van Rensburg win over doubters? Is it Pollock's time to start? Will England play three locks v Boks? Is Caluori ready?
Rugby.body:
England head coach Steve Borthwick has settled on his men for the inaugural Nations Championship, which they kick off against world champions South Africa in Johannesburg on 4 July.

RugbyPass assesses five key issues to emerge from the 36-man squad named on Monday. Resting Maro Itoje is a smart long-term move. He hasn’t been the same force this season on the back of a cumulative rugby workload which would have left Hercules sagging. He has also had to deal with the loss of his mum. Some proper time away from the game will be beneficial for him and England down the line.

Rugby.title: 'If I were coaching him...': Rassie Erasmus likens Henry Pollock to Bok superstar
Rugby.summary: South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus has said that the only thing that counts regarding England back-row Henry Pollock is what he does on the field, describing the 21-year-old's output as "exceptional".
Rugby.body:
South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus has said that the only thing that counts regarding England back-row Henry Pollock is what he does on the field, describing the 21-year-old’s output as “exceptional”.

Fresh from a player of the match performance as Northampton Saints won the Gallagher PREM final against Exeter Chiefs on Saturday, Pollock is expected to play a starring role in England’s Nations Championship campaign, which begins for Steve Borthwick’s side against the Springboks at Ellis Park on July 5.

Rugby.title: Proud Warrior Dobie commits to long-term deal at Scotstoun
Rugby.summary: Scotland scrum-half/wing Jamie Dobie has signed a long-term contract extension with Glasgow Warriors.
Rugby.body:
Scotland scrum-half/wing Jamie Dobie has signed a long-term contract extension with Glasgow Warriors. The new deal, understood to be for three years until the summer of 2029, comes as the 25-year-old approaches a century of games in Glasgow colours, having made 99 appearances in his seven years with the club to date.

Dobie made his senior professional debut in the opening match of the 2019/20 season, coming off the bench away to the Toyota Cheetahs after joining the Warriors directly from Merchiston Castle School.

Rugby.title: England wing Venner to stay at Gloucester-Hartpury
Rugby.summary: England winger Mia Venner signs a new deal to stay with Gloucester-Hartpury.
Rugby.body:
England winger Mia Venner has signed a new deal to stay with Gloucester-Hartpury. Venner joined the Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) side in 2019 and last season played in every one of their games, while her tally of 12 tries is the joint highest in the league.

The 24-year-old has been capped four times by the Red Roses and scored two tries as part of this year's Six Nations Grand Slam-winning squad. "[Mia] brings some outstanding qualities both on and off the field, which make her a hugely valuable member of the squad," said head of rugby Dan Murphy.

Rugby.title: Gloucester Hartpury 'delighted' to retain 'key figure' for 2026/27
Rugby.summary: England international Mia Venner has signed a new deal with her club side Gloucester Hartpury.
Rugby.body:
England Red Roses wing Mia Venner has signed a new deal with her club side Gloucester Hartpury. The 24-year-old first joined the West Country side in 2019 while she was still in school and studied at Hartpury University. As a 17-year the wing, who can also play full-back, scored a hat-trick on her debut for the club.

Later that season she was called into the senior Red Roses’ camp and scored a try on her Test debut against Wales in the 2020 Guinness Women’s Six Nations. This term Venner featured in every Gloucester Hartpury fixture across Premiership Women’s Rugby and the PWR Cup. In the league she scored 10 tries, beat 76 defenders and made 1,066 metres.

Rugby.title: Lake to captain Wales in summer internationals
Rugby.summary: The Gloucester-bound hooker will captain Wales in this summer's four internationals, stating with Saturday's game against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Wales have named hooker Dewi Lake as captain for their summer internationals ahead of fit-again flanker Jac Morgan. Lake led the side for eight Tests in the 2025 autumn series and 2026 Six Nations while Morgan was injured.

The British and Irish Lions flanker captained Wales in the first game of the Steve Tandy era against Argentina but dislocated his left shoulder when scoring a try. The Gloucester-bound Ospreys have both been named in the squad for the summer internationals, which start against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium in Twickenham on Saturday, and Lake will be skipper.

Rugby.title: George North: 'My friends have had rugby taken away
Rugby.summary: The Welsh icon reflects on a glittering career, hoisting Israel Folau and his turmoil with concussion as he prepares to play his final match.
Rugby.body:
Few athletes, no matter how celebrated, are afforded the ultimate send-off, carried from the field on the shoulders of their comrades, medal round the neck, trophy held aloft, sailing into the sunset on an ocean of champagne.

Sitting on the sidelines in the south of France, any sort of farewell at all seemed remote for George North. This great totem of the game was destined to see out his career from the stands as his Provence side came within a match of promotion to the Top 14. For various reasons, North has not played since February. The Welsh icon watched, unfulfilled and stymied, from the periphery and made his peace with the fact this was how it would end.

Rugby.title: Sale Sharks recruit 'most underrated' player in PWR
Rugby.summary: Sale Sharks boss Tom Hudson described new signing Freya Aucken as one of Premiership Women's Rugby's "most underrated" players when the North West club announced her signing.
Rugby.body:
Sale Sharks boss Tom Hudson described new signing Freya Aucken as one of Premiership Women’s Rugby‘s “most underrated” players when the North West club announced her signing. The 24-year-old has joined Sale from Harlequins, who the back three player and scrum-half has represented since the 2020/21 Premier 15s season. She has represented the West London club on 81 occasions and scored five tries in the 2025/26 season.

Hudson said: “Freya is arguably one of the most underrated players in the PWR. “With her ability to be equally at home on the wing or at scrum half and with one PWR title already under her belt we are massively excited to be bringing her North and for her to join our Sharks family. I can’t wait to see what we can achieve together.”

Rugby.title: Top 14 star among several high-profile omissions from Uruguay squad
Rugby.summary: Uruguay have revealed their 33-player roster for the upcoming World Rugby Nations Cup, with the Teros missing several of their main starters.
Rugby.body:
Uruguay have revealed their 33-player roster for the upcoming World Rugby Nations Cup, with the Teros missing several of their main starters. Castres scrum-half Santiago Arata, who was called up by the Barbarians this month, is one of the biggest omissions from the playing squad.

Arata, 29, is one of the few Uruguayans in history to play as a full-time professional in a major European league, and has been capped 42 times. Regular skipper Manuel Leindekar is also among the absentees, with the captain nursing a minor injury and focusing on recovering in time to join his new club, FC Grenoble, for pre-season.

Rugby.title: Antoine Dupont missing for now as Gathie names 33-man France squad
Rugby.summary: Fabien Galthie has named his 33-man France squad for their Nations Championship fixtures against the All Blacks, Australia and Japan next month, with try-scoring maestro Louis Bielle-Biarrey not included.
Rugby.body:
Fabien Galthie has named his 33-man France squad for their Nations Championship fixtures against the All Blacks, Australia and Japan next month, with try-scoring maestro Louis Bielle-Biarrey not included.

The 23-year-old, who followed up being crowned the Six Nations player of the Championship with the Investec player of the year accolade, will be given a rest this summer, which had been widely reported, but a number of his Bordeaux-Begles team-mates have been included following their failure to make the Top 14 play-offs.

Rugby.title: Ex-Wallaby Top 14 winger set to return to rugby league
Rugby.summary: Former Wallaby winger Suliasi Vunivalu, who has been released by La Rochelle, is poised to switch back to rugby league and has been lined up for a move to Super League outfit Hull.
Rugby.body:
Former Wallaby winger Suliasi Vunivalu, who has been released by La Rochelle, is poised to switch back to rugby league and has been lined up for a move to Super League outfit Hull.

Vunivalu, 30, scored two tries in four games before the Champions Cup defeat 42-21 to the Stormers in December, when he ruptured his ACL and Ronan O’Gara called time on his stay on France’s Atlantic coast.

Rugby.title: Winners and losers from Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad
Rugby.summary: Hurricanes trio Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo have receieved their maiden call-up, alongside German-born loose-forward Anton Segner.
Rugby.body:
Rennie’s 34-man squad was named on Monday at Fielding Rugby Club in Manawatu, ahead of three Nations Championship fixtures in New Zealand, starting on July 4. The new selection panel, including Rennie, Sir Graham Henry, and senior assistant Neil Barnes, picked four debutants in their first squad of 2026.

Hurricanes trio Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo received their maiden call-up, alongside German-born loose-forward Anton Segner. The four inclusions have squeezed out a number of experienced All Blacks, and players who many expected to be named.

Hurricanes trio Xavier Numia, Josh Moorby, and Fehi Fineanganofo received their maiden call-up, alongside German-born loose-forward Anton Segner. The four inclusions have squeezed out a number of experienced All Blacks, and players who many expected to be named.

Rugby.title: 'We are building something genuinely special': Red Roses agree new contract framework with RFU
Rugby.summary: The RFU and England's Red Roses have agreed a new four-year contract framework that covers the current Women's Rugby World Cup cycle.
Rugby.body:
The RFU and England’s Red Roses have agreed a new four-year contract framework that covers the current Women’s Rugby World Cup cycle. English rugby’s governing body and the world champion side worked collaboratively with Team England Rugby to agree the new framework, which exists between 2026 and 2030.

In their press release, the RFU said: “This agreement represents a significant further investment in England’s women’s rugby programme, reinforcing the RFU’s long-term commitment to building a world-leading performance environment while accelerating the global growth of the women’s game.”

Rugby.title: Attack coach Gleeson leaves Bath
Rugby.summary: Bath attack coach Martin Gleeson leaves the club after eight months in the role.
Rugby.body:
Bath backs and attack coach Martin Gleeson has left the club after eight months in the role. Gleeson, a former England and Great Britain rugby league centre and two-time Super League winner, joined Bath in October a week after he left a coaching role at Warrington Wolves.

"I'd like to thank Johann for the opportunity this season to come to Bath and work with some amazing staff and talented players," Gleeson said. "It's been a great season and I wish the club all the success in the future."

Rugby.title: Quins poach Super Rugby champions' attack coach
Rugby.summary: Harlequins appoint Brad Cooper as their new attack coach, as they reshape their backroom staff for the 2026-27 season.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins have appointed Brad Cooper as their new attack coach, as they continue to reshape their backroom staff for the 2026-27 season. Cooper joins from Super Rugby champions Hurricanes, who averaged 41 points per game during the regular season, and scored 114 tries on their way to the trophy.

"It's clear there's a real ambition here to play rugby with tempo, and that's exactly the environment I want to be part of," Cooper told the club's website, external. "This is a great chance to help shape an attacking identity at a club with such history, and test myself as a coach in the northern hemisphere in the Gallagher Prem."

Rugby.title: Red Roses stars in line for £100K to retain World Cup
Rugby.summary: The Red Roses' top stars stand to earn £100,000 with England in 2029 should they successfully defend their Women's Rugby World Cup title in Australia.
Rugby.body:
The Red Roses' top stars stand to earn £100,000 with England in 2029 should they successfully defend their Women's Rugby World Cup title in Australia as part of a new four-year pay agreement with the Rugby Football Union.

Overall, it is understood the players have negotiated a 25% increase on their previous terms that will expire at the end of June. In addition to lifting the World Cup in front of a sellout 81,885 crowd in September, England have won eight successive Six Nations titles and are on a run of 38 successive Test victories.

Rugby.title: Bath Rugby have a coaching spot to fill after departure confirmed
Rugby.summary: Bath Rugby have confirmed that backs and attack coach Martin Gleeson has left the club after only one season in the role.
Rugby.body:
Bath Rugby have confirmed that backs and attack coach Martin Gleeson has left the club after only one season in the role. Gleeson joined Bath in October 2025, once Lee Blackett started work as England’s new attack coach.

While 102 tries were scored in the Prem season, matching the previous season’s return, Bath’s attack didn’t seem to fire as well under the rugby league legend, who is now looking for his next coaching gig.

Rugby.title: Faf de Klerk set for immediate international return after Springboks axe
Rugby.summary: South Africa scrum-half Faf de Klerk has been named in the Barbarians matchday squad to take on Wales at Twickenham's Allianz Stadium on Saturday, just days after missing out on Rassie Erasmus' 46-man Springboks squad for the Nations Championship.
Rugby.body:
South Africa scrum-half Faf de Klerk has been named in the Barbarians matchday squad to take on Wales at Twickenham’s Allianz Stadium on Saturday, just days after missing out on Rassie Erasmus’ 46-man Springboks squad for the Nations Championship.

The 34-year-old featured from the bench in the Boks’ 80-31 win over the BaaBaas at the Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium last Saturday, but failed to make the cut for their squad, which was named soon after.

Rugby.title: Bristol sign Wales back row Lewis
Rugby.summary: Bristol Bears sign Wales back row forward Bethan Lewis for the 2026-27 season following her departure from Gloucester-Hartpury.
Rugby.body:
Bristol Bears have signed Wales back row forward Bethan Lewis for the 2026-27 season following her departure from Gloucester-Hartpury. Lewis has been capped 65 times by Wales and captained the squad during this year's Women's Six Nations.

The 27-year-old won three Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) titles with Gloucester-Hartpury, having joined the club in 2018 where she made 95 appearances. "The club has a clear vision for where it wants to go and after speaking with Scott [Lawson] and the coaching team, it is a journey that I'm keen to be a part of," Lewis said.

Rugby.title: ‘Two formats, one system’: Walsh hungry to bring winning sevens culture to Wallaroos
Rugby.summary: Few individuals have spent more time as involved with Australian women’s rugby than Tim Walsh, the head coach first getting his start with the sevens side in 2013, being at the head of some of Australian rugby’s biggest successes over the last decade.
Rugby.body:
Few individuals have spent more time as involved with Australian women’s rugby than Tim Walsh. The 47-year-old got his start with the sevens team in 2013 and has been at the heart of Australian rugby’s biggest success over the past decade.

Now, as Rugby Australia’s new Director of Women’s High Performance, Walsh has an opportunity to transform the footprint of Australian women’s rugby for generations to come. Australian women’s rugby is set for a colossal six years. The Aussie Women’s Sevens, fresh off their 2026 HSBC SVNS World Championship win, are firmly focused on the 2028 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles and a home Games in Brisbane in 2032.

Rugby.title: All Blacks 2IC on big shift coaches are making with 2026 squad
Rugby.summary: The new All Blacks coaching regime is looking to reverse a trend it has identified in the country's style of play, with Monday's squad selection key to that shift.
Rugby.body:
The new All Blacks coaching regime is looking to reverse a trend it has identified in the country’s style of play, with Monday’s squad selection key to that reversal. The team’s 2IC, Neil Barnes, says there will be a renewed focus on tackle evasion and on using skill sets in the All Blacks’ attack, rather than an over-dedication to winning collisions, which he says detracts from New Zealand’s traditional strengths.

Barnes joined Dave Rennie’s coaching ticket three months prior to the first All Blacks squad announcement of 2026, with the head coach winning his position, in large part, thanks to what he described as a clear vision for how he wants the team to play. That clarity gave selectors – Rennie, Barnes, and Sir Graham Henry – the bulk of the Super Rugby Pacific season to find the best players to bring that vision to life.

Rugby.title: Neil Barnes on tightest All Blacks selections: 'You don't do s**t like that lightly'
Rugby.summary: All Blacks lead assistant coach Neil Barnes has shared some insights into the tight selection calls in the first All Blacks squad of 2026, addressing the established Test players who have been left out.
Rugby.body:
All Blacks lead assistant coach Neil Barnes has shared some insights into the tight selection calls in the first All Blacks squad of 2026, addressing the established Test players who have been left out.

Some of the names fans were most shocked not to see in Monday’s squad included Rieko Ioane and Du’Plessis Kirifi, both in their prime playing years and were All Blacks in 2025.

Rugby.title: '10 games off the bench': Ex-All Black's theory on Kyle Preston selection
Rugby.summary: One of the most surprising names in Dave Rennie's first All Black squad was not a debutant, but second year halfback Kyle Preston from the Crusaders.
Rugby.body:
One of the most surprising names in Dave Rennie’s first All Black squad was not a debutant, but second year halfback Kyle Preston from the Crusaders. The three halfbacks who made the cut were Hurricanes star Cam Roigard, Chiefs’ Cortez Ratima and Kyle Preston.

Preston beat out a handful of capped All Black No.9s, including his club teammate at the Crusaders Noah Hotham who had started all year ahead of him in Christchurch. Blues halfback Finlay Christie, with 28-Test caps, also missed out. Christie started the Eden Park Test against the Springboks last year, which was Preston’s All Black debut where he had a handful of minutes off the bench.

Rugby.title: 'I'm very content': Crusaders capatin David Havili announces departure
Rugby.summary: David Havili's time in red and black has come to an end after one of the most successful Super Rugby careers in the competition's history.
Rugby.body:
David Havili’s time in red and black has come to an end after one of the most successful Super Rugby careers in the competition’s history. Departing the club after two seasons as captain, Havili was involved in all eight of the Crusaders’ recent championship seasons, starting at fullback in four finals and second five-eighth in three, while missing the 2023 final through an injury suffered just ahead of the playoffs.

Having debuted for the club in 2015, the 31-year-old has 155 Crusaders caps to his name and was just the eighth player, and first back, to be named Crusaders captain, following in the footsteps of Richard Loe, Todd Blackadder, Reuben Thorne, Richie McCaw, Kieran Read, Sam Whitelock, and Scott Barrett.

Rugby.title: Mike Blair on Kirifi: 'There's some pretty impressive players in front of him'
Rugby.summary: Both players had exceptional seasons, but Rennie and his coaching staff opted for the German-born Blues No.7, over the eight Test All Black.
Rugby.body:
While Dave Rennie had the opportunity to give Anton Segner a maiden All Blacks call-up, there was also a difficult conversation to be had with Hurricanes captain Du’Plessis Kirifi.

Segner, one of the four uncapped players to be named in Monday’s 34-man squad, was likely battling Kirifi for one spot in Rennie’s loose-forward mix. Despite Kirifi co-captaining his side to a Super Rugby Pacific title, assistant coach Mike Blair said that the loose-forward position was tough to pick, and there are a number of players who missed out.

Rugby.title: Veteran Black Ferns lock signs fresh extension before Lions Tour
Rugby.summary: "It’s great to see our women’s players making long term commitments to the game as we look forward to an exciting international calendar over the next three years.”
Rugby.body:
Veteran Black Ferns lock Maiakawanakaulani Roos is sticking around for the next two years, penning an extension with New Zealand Rugby ahead of the historic British and Irish Lions Tour on Kiwi shores.

The 41-Test Black Fern has been a staple for the national side since making her debut back in 2021, scoring three tries in her international career to date. Roos said signing an extension was important to her, as the Black Ferns and Blues environments provided her with an opportunity to begin her career.

Rugby.title: Does one size really fit all in the Wallabies second row?
Rugby.summary: Lukhan Salakaia-Loto's omission from their latest squad suggests the impact of the 'very big men' is under-valued in Australia.
Rugby.body:
The late Karl Lagerfeld once memorably proclaimed, “No one wants to see curvy women”. It was a cri de guerre in a fashion industry which wanted to create a sense of exclusivity in the clothes it marketed. You had to be skinny and body-perfect to wear the garments it manufactured. If you were oversized or plus-sized or just oddly-shaped, you could forget it.

It can be the same in sports too. In the Major League Baseball sea-change depicted in Moneyball, the general manager of the Oakland A’s, Billy Beane, launches a major assault on the ‘good body nonsense’ which judges a player’s value on his physical build and athletic tools:

Rugby.title: Lewis crosses West Country divide as three more players exit Gloucester Hartpury
Rugby.summary: Wales flanker Bethan Lewis has traded Gloucester Hartpury for Bristol Bears for the 2026/27 Premiership Women's Rugby season.
Rugby.body:
Wales flanker Bethan Lewis has traded Gloucester Hartpury for Bristol Bears ahead of the 2026/27 Premiership Women’s Rugby season. The 65-cap international, who captained Sean Lynn’s team for the majority of the 2026 Guinness Women’s Six Nations, made 96 appearances across eight years for the three-time PWR champions.

Lewis joins Bristol as an almost like-for-like replacement for Alisha Butchers, who has returned to Wales to play her domestic rugby, and will bring her wealth of experience to Scott Lawson’s side next season.

Lewis joins Bristol as an almost like-for-like replacement for Alisha Butchers, who has returned to Wales to play her domestic rugby, and will bring her wealth of experience to Scott Lawson’s side next season.

Rugby.title: Injured Dombrandt set for long spell on sidelines
Rugby.summary: Harlequins' Alex Dombrandt faces a lengthy absence with an ACL injury sustained in England's international against a France XV.
Rugby.body:
England number eight Alex Dombrandt faces a "prolonged period" on the sidelines after suffering a serious knee injury in the international against a France XV on Friday. The Harlequins captain, 29, sustained an anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injury during the non-Test summer warm-up, which England lost 35-19.

A club statement read, external: "Alex will be assessed by a specialist in the coming days, before undergoing surgery and then beginning rehabilitation with the club's medical team.

Rugby.title: Shock in U20: Georgia make rugby history at expense of giants of the sport
Rugby.summary: Georgia's Junior Lelos have defeated the New Zealand Junior All Blacks for the first time in their history, securing a dramatic 22-19 victory at the Kharebi Arena in Rustavi.
Rugby.body:
Georgia’s Junior Lelos have defeated the New Zealand Junior All Blacks for the first time in their history, securing a dramatic 22-19 victory at the Kharebi Arena in Rustavi. Vito Kolelishvili’s team scored four tries against their highly fancied opponents, the last of which came in the final minute to seal a famous win in their last warm-up match before the 2026 World Rugby U20 Championship begins on June 27.

The hosts had previously faced New Zealand four times at the U20 level, losing on each occasion, but they showed immense character to overcome an early deficit and a late fightback from the visitors.

Rugby.title: Itoje rested as Caluori among five England call-ups
Rugby.summary: England captain Maro Itoje will miss England's Test against South Africa on 4 July after being left out of the entirety of the summer's Nations Championship action.
Rugby.body:
England captain Maro Itoje will miss England's Test against South Africa on 4 July after being left out of this summer's Nations Championship action. The 31-year-old has been rested, having breached World Rugby's recommendation , externalof playing only 30 games a season in each of his past two campaigns.

In addition to leading England and club side Saracens, Itoje skippered the British and Irish Lions in their series win over Australia last summer. He has also grieved his mother Florence who died in December, speaking at her funeral in Nigeria and, by arrangement with the national team, joined up late with his team-mates before the Six Nations.
Rugby.copy:
England captain Maro Itoje will miss England's Test against South Africa on 4 July after being left out of this summer's Nations Championship action. The 31-year-old has been rested, having breached World Rugby's recommendation , externalof playing only 30 games a season in each of his past two campaigns.

In addition to leading England and club side Saracens, Itoje skippered the British and Irish Lions in their series win over Australia last summer. He has also grieved his mother Florence who died in December, speaking at her funeral in Nigeria and, by arrangement with the national team, joined up late with his team-mates before the Six Nations.

Rugby.title: England XV outing proves costly for Alex Dombrandt
Rugby.summary: Harlequins have confirmed that captain Alex Dombrandt is set for a lengthy spell on the sidelines after suffering an ACL injury whilst playing for England XV on Friday night.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins have confirmed that captain Alex Dombrandt is set for a lengthy spell on the sidelines after suffering an ACL injury whilst playing for England XV on Friday night. Dombrandt was a shining light in an otherwise disappointing season for the London club, and had forced his way back into the senior England reckoning.

The 29-year-old, who was last capped 12 months ago against the USA, was named in England’s training squad for the upcoming Nations Championship, but was left out of the final 36-player group following the injury against France XV in Vannes.

Rugby.title: 'Freak' athlete Ted Hill earns place in England's Nations Championship squad
Rugby.summary: The squad, which includes five uncapped players – Noah Caluori, Greg Fisilau, Benhard Janse van Rensburg, George Kloska and Vilikesa Sela – will train at England’s Performance Centre at Pennyhill Park until Wednesday.
Rugby.body:
Bath loose forward Ted Hill has been rewarded for his strong showing for England XV vs their French counterparts on Friday night with a place in Steve Borthwick’s 36-player squad for the inaugural Nations Championship.

Hill, who was once described by club captain Ben Spencer as ‘a freak’, due to his physical attributes and pace, wasn’t included in the original training group, but is now on course to make his Test comeback.

Rugby.title: USA Women's Eagles trio top list of 10 Leicester Tigers departures
Rugby.summary: Leicester Tigers have confirmed the impending departure of 10 players from Mattioli Woods Welford Road at the end of the Premiership Women's Rugby season.
Rugby.body:
Leicester Tigers have confirmed the impending departure of 10 players from the East Midlands at the end of the Premiership Women’s Rugby season. These departures include USA Women’s Eagles trio Emily Henrich, Kristin Bitter and Emerson Allen. All three players represented their country at Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025. Henrich first joined Leicester in 2024 and scored 14 tries in her debut season.

The 26-year-old registered two tries in 15 PWR appearances in 2025/26, Bitter played 16 matches this season and Allen played in 14 fixtures. Wales international Katherine Baverstock exits Mattioli Woods Welford Road after one season. Due to injury the 23-year-old prop only made six appearances all season for Tigers as they finished bottom of the PWR table.

Rugby.title: 'It feels like the cherry on top for me': Maxwell determined to enjoy final game
Rugby.summary: Jenny Maxwell says playing for the Barbarians next weekend will be the perfect “cherry on top” of her stellar rugby career and she cannot wait to pull the boots back on one last time.
Rugby.body:
Jenny Maxwell says playing for the Barbarians next weekend will be the perfect “cherry on top” of her stellar rugby career and she cannot wait to pull the boots back on one last time.

The Barbarians men have been known for years as a team that play with flair and, since its formation in 2017, the Barbarians women’s team has done the same. On Saturday 27 June, the men will take on Wales at 2pm at Allianz Stadium, Twickenham, in West London with the women taking on their Welsh counterparts at 5pm in a hotly anticipated double header.

Rugby.title: Mick Cleary: 'Henry Pollock has had his brickbats, so it’s only right he gets plaudits for how he's knuckled down'
Rugby.summary: Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs served up a swashbuckling Gallagher Prem Final as a template for Steve Borthwick to follow
Rugby.body:
There were eyeballs elsewhere, across America at the World Cup, across London at the Oval and at Queen’s, yet the PREM proved that it too has currency in the sporting market with an 82,000 full house drawn to the Allianz Stadium, Twickenham. As the sparsely populated stands for the URC final on Friday night at Croke Park showed, none of this should be taken for granted. The punter holds all the cards: if he or she fancies what is on offer then they will come. They came to South-West London, from all corners of the land as the mixed-bag colours in the stands showed because they had faith in what was on offer. It was a day in the sun although the weather alone is a fickle part of the package. Year on year spectators know that they will get hammer-and-tongs rugby to round off the season. It is early summer’s farewell to an arduous nine-month campaign.

As with the match itself, the action across the winter months was imperfect, flawed in its error-count but never less than whole-hearted and intense. There is much yet to correct with the PREM – too few teams, no jeopardy at the bottom, too many blow-out scores – but when the end product is what Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs served up on Saturday afternoon, then the heart of the sport can be seen to be beating strongly, even if we all must guard against those damned mid-half hydration breaks becoming a TV ad-man’s opportunity as it is proving at the FIFA World Cup.

Rugby.title: Huw Jones: Leaving Glasgow 'a difficult decision, but also an easy decision'
Rugby.summary: Huw Jones has admitted that leaving Glasgow was a wrench but says the history and stature of Toulon made it an ultimately straightforward call.
Rugby.body:
Huw Jones has admitted that leaving Glasgow was a wrench but says the history and stature of Toulon made it an ultimately straightforward call. The Scotland centre visited his new club ahead of his move to the Top 14 giants next season, and came away with his decision firmly vindicated.

“Really cool to be here,” Jones told the club website. “This is my first time visiting Toulon. Got to have a look around at the town and see the campus. The facility is amazing.”

Rugby.title: Stuart McCloskey commits to Ulster after standout season
Rugby.summary: The Ireland centre has penned a new one-year deal, extending his stay with the Irish province until 2028.
Rugby.body:
Ulster have secured the services of Stuart McCloskey until at least 2028, with the Ireland centre signing a new one-year contract extension. McCloskey, 33, enjoyed a standout 2025/26 season which resulted in a nomination for this year’s Guinness Men’s Six Nations Player of the Championship.

He started all five of Ireland’s games at inside centre and led the way for try assists (six), dominant contacts (18), turnovers won (8) and minutes played. With 217 appearances for Ulster, the former Dungannon RFC player is on the verge of equalling the third most senior Ulster appearances ever (218) alongside former captain, Rory Best.

Rugby.title: Baxter urges Ridl to build on impressive season
Rugby.summary: Exeter Chiefs boss Rob Baxter says top try-scorer Campbell Ridl must ensure he does not become a "one-shot wonder".
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs boss Rob Baxter says top try-scorer Campbell Ridl must ensure he does not become a "one-shot wonder". The 21-year-old winger capped his first campaign in Exeter's senior side with a try and a try-saving tackle in their Prem final loss to Northampton Saints on Saturday.

It capped a breakout season for the England Under-20 player, who scored 17 tries in all competitions for Exeter this season, but Baxter said Ridl must build on his successful first season at Sandy Park.

Rugby.title: 'I need to focus on giving back': Firefighter Wales prop announces Test retirement
Rugby.summary: Wales' Jenni Scoble will retire from international rugby to focus on her career with the Fire Service after this weekend's Barbarians feature.
Rugby.body:
Wales‘ Jenni Scoble will retire from international rugby to focus on her career with the Fire Service after this weekend’s Barbarians fixture. The 33-year-old prop will play her final game for Wales this weekend at Allianz Stadium and will continue to play for Gwalia Lightning in the Celtic Challenge, while also working as a full-time firefighter.

Scoble made her Test debut against Australia in 2024 at WXV2 and was a member of Sean Lynn’s Women’s Rugby World Cup squad in 2025. She has won eight caps for her country. The eight-cap international scored her first international try for Wales against England at the Principality Stadium in the 2025 edition of the Guinness Women’s Six Nations.

Rugby.title: Savea new All Blacks captain as Fineanganofo in squad
Rugby.summary: Ardie Savea has been made the All Blacks captain in coach Dave Rennie's first squad announcement, while Newcastle-bound winger Fehi Fineanganofo gets a first call-up.
Rugby.body:
Ardie Savea has been made the All Blacks captain in coach Dave Rennie's first squad announcement. The 32-year-old takes over from Scott Barrett, who skippered the side during Scott Robertson's tenure, but has been sidelined with back surgery.

"I believe this role is about empowering everyone in the group to be the best that they can be - from the leadership, to the players and wider staff," said loose-forward Savea.

Rugby.title: Rassie Erasmus defends Bok star after 'worst player on the pitch' jibe
Rugby.summary: Rassie Erasmus has dismissed criticism of experienced outside half Handré Pollard after the 32-year-old struggled in the URC playoffs for the Bulls, which saw them lose 36-7 in the final to Leinster.
Rugby.body:
Rassie Erasmus has dismissed criticism of experienced outside half Handré Pollard after the 32-year-old struggled in the URC playoffs for the Bulls, which saw them lose 36-7 in the final to Leinster.

His support is timely given the flak aimed his way by former Irish star and now leading pundit Simon Zebo, who stated after the final: “Pollard, a World Cup winner. He’s been the worst player on the pitch. Canan Moodie, you could say the same thing about him. You rely on your best players to step up in big games. They have been nothing short of horrible.”

Rugby.title: Cullen to leave Leinster at end of next season
Rugby.summary: Leinster head coach Leo Cullen is to leave his role with the Irish province at the end of next season.
Rugby.body:
Leo Cullen is to leave his role as Leinster head coach at the end of next season. The former Ireland second row will depart after 12 years in the position with the Irish province when his contract concludes at the end of the 2026-27 campaign, a statement from Leinster confirmed on Monday.

The 48-year-old has led Leinster to six league titles during his tenure, the latest coming in the United Rugby Championship decider against the Bulls on Friday. He also guided the province to a European Champions Cup success in 2018 but his side subsequently lost five finals in the competition, including four in the past five years.

Rugby.title: All Blacks call-up Fehi Fineanganofo casts major doubt on Newcastle Red Bulls move
Rugby.summary: Fehi Fineanganofo has cast major doubt over his impending move to the Newcastle Red Bulls following his recent selection for the All Blacks.
Rugby.body:
Fehi Fineanganofo has cast major doubt over his impending move to the Newcastle Red Bulls following his recent selection for the All Blacks. The 23-year-old Hurricanes winger, who enjoyed a breakthrough season by equalling the Super Rugby Pacific record with 17 tries, was named as one of four uncapped players in Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad for the upcoming Nations Championship.

Fineanganofo had originally signed a two-year deal with the Gallagher PREM side earlier this year, a move that was expected to pause his international ambitions in New Zealand.

Rugby.title: Firefighting prop Scoble set to play last Wales game
Rugby.summary: Firefighter Jenni Scoble is set to play her final game for Wales against the Barbarians at Twickenham on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Firefighting Wales prop Jenni Scoble will retire from international rugby after the fixture against Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday. The 33-year-old is set to make one last outing for her country in the double-header in Twickenham, with the women's game taking place at 17:00 BST after the men play at 14:00 BST.

Scoble will continue to play for Gwalia Lightning in the Celtic Challenge but has opted to retire from international rugby to focus on her job as a firefighter. "This has been an incredibly tough decision to make and is one I have thought long and hard about," said Scoble.

Rugby.title: Prem Rugby play-offs set to move to neutral venue
Rugby.summary: The Prem Rugby play-offs are set to move from home advantage to a neutral venue from the 2029-2030 season.
Rugby.body:
The Prem Rugby play-offs are set be played at a neutral venue from the 2029-2030 season. The current format, which sees the top two teams in the regular season table getting home advantage in the semi-finals, has been in place since 2005.

But league bosses say they have a vision of playing both the play-offs on consecutive days in one city, similar to the European finals weekend. It is thought Liverpool and Brighton are two of the cities under consideration at this early stage.

Rugby.title: Tamati Ellison names 26-man Maori All Blacks squad for Japan XV clash
Rugby.summary: Ellison said that naming his first squad is a proud moment, and that all of the players have earned their opportunity.
Rugby.body:
Maori All Blacks head coach Tamati Ellison has named his 26 man squad for this weekend’s encounter with Japan XV at Paloma Mizuho Stadium, in Nagoya. The former All Blacks assistant, who left his role alongside Scott Robertson, Bryn Evans and Scott Hansen, has picked a strong squad to head to Japan, helped by assistant coaches Greg Feek (Kahungunu) and Marty Bourke (Tainui).

Ellison has named six uncapped players as part of the 2026 squad. The possible debutants include, JJ Pokai, Reon Paul, Torian Barnes, Adam Lennox, Payton Spencer and Tahlor Cahill.

Rugby.title: All Blacks head coach details what made Anton Segner impossible to leave out
Rugby.summary: "We think he could generally be a high quality seven, but you know he's got a really balanced skill set."
Rugby.body:
The big inclusion in Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad of 2026 was German-born Blues loose-forward Anton Segner, who had a standout season for the Auckland-based franchise.

The Blues loose-forward is versatile, can play all three positions in the back-row, and his form in the 2026 Super Rugby Pacific season has given him an opportunity in Rennie’s first squad.

Rugby.title: Tandy's Wales prepare for challenging summer of travel
Rugby.summary: Welsh domestic rugby might have finished at the end of May but there is very little rest for the Wales national men's side this summer.
Rugby.body:
Welsh domestic rugby might have finished at the end of May but there is very little rest for the national men's side. Steve Tandy's Wales team face four matches in successive weekends with the rugby season lasting until mid-July.

The uncapped men's and women's double-header against Barbarians on 27 June at the Allianz Stadium, will be followed by three games in the new Nations Championship against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa in July.
Rugby.copy:
Welsh domestic rugby might have finished at the end of May but there is very little rest for the national men's side. Steve Tandy's Wales team face four matches in successive weekends with the rugby season lasting until mid-July.

The uncapped men's and women's double-header against Barbarians on 27 June at the Allianz Stadium, will be followed by three games in the new Nations Championship against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa in July.

Rugby.title: Dave Rennie on the Hurricanes' pair of wingers and the four potential debutants
Rugby.summary: Dave Rennie's first All Blacks squad includes four potential debutants, including three from the Super Rugby champion Hurricanes squad.
Rugby.body:
Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad includes four potential debutants, including three from the Super Rugby champion Hurricanes squad. Blues loose forward Anton Segner, Hurricanes prop Xavier Numia, and the pair of record-setting Hurricanes wingers Fehi Fineanganofo and Josh Moorby are the four rookies in line to become All Blacks.

Fineanganofo and Moorby set a new single season try scoring record with 17 tries each in the record-setting championship season. Doubt still surrounds Fehi Fineanganofo’s long-term future in New Zealand, but while he is contracted to NZR until the end of November, remains eligible to play.

Rugby.title: Ex-All Blacks left shocked over Du'Plessis Kirifi omission from All Blacks squad
Rugby.summary: Ex-All Blacks James Parsons and Jeff Wilson have been left shocked at the omission of Hurricanes openside flanker Du'Plessis Kirifi.
Rugby.body:
Ex-All Blacks James Parsons and Jeff Wilson have been left shocked at the omission of Hurricanes openside flanker Du’Plessis Kirifi in the first All Blacks squad of 2026. After the co-captain lead the Hurricanes to a comprehensive 60-5 win over the Chiefs in the Super Rugby Pacific final, Kirifi was one of the notable omissions from Dave Rennie’s first squad.

Parsons said that Kirifi “could not have done anymore” as a player after his most impressive season yet with the Hurricanes, and the selection of Savea as captain suggests that he will be an 80-minute player.
