Rugby.title: Springbok Grant Williams' new club confirmed as 19-cap All Black joins him
Rugby.summary: Japan Rugby League One champions Kobelco Kobe Steelers have moved quickly to strengthen their squad ahead of the 2026/27 season, confirming the signings of South Africa's Grant Williams and former All Black George Bridge.
Rugby.body:
Newly-crowned Japan Rugby League One champions Kobelco Kobe Steelers have moved quickly to strengthen their squad ahead of the 2026/27 season, confirming the signings of South Africa’s Grant Williams and former All Black George Bridge.

The Steelers were crowned champions earlier this month after beating Kubota Spears 22-13 in the final, but will begin the defence of their title under new leadership following Dave Rennie’s departure to take charge of New Zealand. Former All Blacks assistant Wayne Smith is set to take over at the Kobe-based club.

Rugby.title: Furbank targets fairytale end to Northampton career
Rugby.summary: Captain George Furbank is targeting a "fairytale finish" with Northampton as his decade at the club ends in Saturday's Prem final at the Allianz Stadium in Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
Captain George Furbank is targeting a "fairytale finish" with Northampton as his decade at the club ends on Saturday at the Allianz Stadium in Twickenham. Saints legend Furbank will leave his boyhood team to join Harlequins next season after failing to agree a new deal at Franklin's Gardens.

The 29-year-old full-back will lead the side out in the Prem final against Exeter, and says there are no hard feelings despite the "tough conversations" around the contract stand-off.

The 29-year-old full-back will lead the side out in the Prem final against Exeter, and says there are no hard feelings despite the "tough conversations" around the contract stand-off.

Rugby.title: Irish-qualified 16-cap England star among two incoming Munster signings
Rugby.summary: Munster head coach Clayton McMillan is closing in on making two more signings ahead of next season with Australian playmaker Will Harrison and former England tighthead Kieran Brookes set to sign for the province.
Rugby.body:
Munster head coach Clayton McMillan is closing in on making two more signings ahead of next season with Australian playmaker Will Harrison and former England tighthead Kieran Brookes set to sign for the province.

McMillan has been searching the globe for a new fly-half, and The42 is reporting that they have finally found their man in Harrison, who scored three tries in eight games for Hanazono Kintetsu Liners in Japan’s second division this season.

Rugby.title: Where to Watch: 'Le Petit Crunch'
Rugby.summary: The summer of international rugby starts with a bang in Vannes on Friday night, as France and England XVs stacked with talent go head to head in what amounts to a Test match in all but name.
Rugby.body:
The summer of international rugby starts with a bang in Vannes on Friday night, as France and England XVs stacked with talent go head to head in what amounts to a Test match in all but name.

Serving as a final trial before the start of the inaugural Nations Championship, the clash between two of rugby’s fiercest rivals has no end of storylines. From an England perspective, all eyes will be on Saracens sensation Noah Caluori as he looks to put the icing on a superb domestic season, which ended with him being the Prem’s top try scorer with 18, while Bath centre Max Ojomoh will be determined to show his Guinness Men’s Nations snub was unjustified.

Rugby.title: Scarlets keep hold of another Wales international
Rugby.summary: Scarlets have continued their retention drive ahead of next season by securing Wales tighthead Harri O’Connor on a new contract.
Rugby.body:
Scarlets have continued their retention drive ahead of next season by securing Wales tighthead Harri O’Connor on a new contract. The 25-year-old has become a regular member of the Scarlets front-row since making his senior debut in 2020, racking up 56 appearances after progressing through the club’s academy.

O’Connor has also broken into the Wales set-up, earning five Test caps since making his debut against Italy during the 2024 Six Nations. The prop is part of a growing group of homegrown talent at Parc y Scarlets, with younger brother Sam also now a member of the senior squad after the pair came through the pathway together. He is latest player to commit his future to the Llanelli-based region, following No.8 Fletcher Anderson, captain Josh Macleod, scrum-half Gareth Davies, full-back Jac Davies, flanker Dan Davis and lock Sam Lousi in signing new contracts.

Rugby.title: Highlanders continue re-signing spree with two ex-U20 stars and Pumas lock
Rugby.summary: The Highlanders' 2027 squad is coming together, with three new re-signings following the five that were announced last week.
Rugby.body:
The Highlanders’ 2027 squad is coming together, with three new re-signings following the five that were announced last week. The club has secured two of its future stars and its most experienced 2026 squad member in the latest run of extensions, with one of the most hyped prospects in New Zealand secured through until 2029.

That three-year deal belongs to halfback Dylan Pledger, who is yet to make his Highlanders debut due to a pre-season ACL tear, but has been backed as the club’s next big thing after two standout New Zealand U20 campaigns and an eye-catching debut season for Otago in the 2025 NPC.

Rugby.title: ‘A Northampton win should be seen as vindication of the burgeoning England credentials of Dowson and Vesty’
Rugby.summary: If the Borthwick Project were to hit the buffers anytime soon, England should look to the driving forces behind Northampton's success.
Rugby.body:
Who needs Andy Farrell? Or Shaun Edwards, fine men both and top coaches in their own field? To judge by the clamour for the RFU to move in on both individuals with the utmost haste – too late in Farrell’s case – you’d think that there wasn’t a host of nearer-to-home options as England look to nail down their succession planning in case the Borthwick Project were to hit the buffers, either this summer or in the build-up towards next year’s World Cup in Australia.

Don’t miss it: France XV v England XV – live and free on RugbyPass TV & RugbyPass app. 17:15 BST, 19 June 2026. While Farrell and Edwards claim the headlines, the fact of the matter is that we have some of the country’s finest on show at Twickenham on Saturday for the PREM final – Phil Dowson and Sam Vesty of Northampton Saints and Exeter’s old slugger, Rob Baxter. Would any England supporter raise a critical eyebrow if either the Saints pair or Baxter were in charge of national affairs post-RWC2027? I doubt you’d find a dissenting voice.

Rugby.title: Entertaining like no other
Rugby.summary: BBC Radio Northampton's Graham McKechnie reflects on the differences between Northampton Saints' 2024 title winning squad and this year's.
Rugby.body:
On Saturday, Northampton Saints return to Twickenham, looking to be crowned as Prem champions for the second time in three seasons. They go there having finished top of the table, scoring more tries in a season than any other club. They have entertained like no other club – the rugby at the Gardens this season has been breathtaking.

One more win will confirm this to be a truly great era for Saints. Two years ago the story was about a young squad reaching its first major final. Young, but with a few notable exceptions.

Rugby.title: Who are the three uncapped players in Ireland squad?
Rugby.summary: BBC Sport NI assesses the credentials of the three uncapped players named by Ireland head coach Andy Farrell in his squad for the upcoming Nations Championship fixtures.
Rugby.body:
The sense of anticipation felt by Ireland supporters about the start of the inaugural Nations Championship ramped up a degree or two on Wednesday when head coach Andy Farrell named his 36-man squad for the three matches his side will play in July.

Farrell's selection for the games against Australia, Japan and New Zealand features three players who have yet to win their first caps - and all three are Connacht forwards. Props Billy Bohan and Sam Illo, along with back row Sean Jansen, are part of the group which will travel to Sydney on Monday to begin their preparation for the three Test matches in the southern hemisphere.

Rugby.title: Force land aspiring Aussie playmaker as Reds sign another Wallaby lock
Rugby.summary: Welsh club Ospreys have continued to raid Australian talent, while an emerging playmaker will head west to escape a Super Rugby Pacific flyhalf logjam.
Rugby.body:
Welsh club Ospreys have continued to raid Australian talent, while an emerging playmaker will head west to escape a Super Rugby Pacific flyhalf logjam. Creighton began his career at the Queensland Reds before moving south last season and spending time in the No.10 and centres.

His departure from Ballymore came as Harry McLaughlin-Phillips emerged. The 22-year-old flyhalf had been linked to a move last season before playing 11 games this year with Reds teammates Carter Gordon and Tom Lynagh battling injury.

Rugby.title: There are only so many former glories that All Blacks selectors can cling to
Rugby.summary: I think we’d all agree that Beauden Barrett will be in the first All Blacks test squad of the season. Just as, we’d also have to admit he probably has no business being in the team anymore.
Rugby.body:
I think we’d all agree that Beauden Barrett will be in the first All Blacks test squad of the season. Just as, we’d also have to admit he probably has no business being in the team anymore.

The justifications for continuing to pick Barrett are obvious: talent, experience, leadership. The guy has been a fixture in the squad since 2012 and often justifiably so. At various times, Barrett has been the best and most influential player in the world, wowing all and sundry with his audacity and skill.

Rugby.title: Brett Cameron weighs in on the No.10 battle in the Super Rugby Pacific final
Rugby.summary: The two perceived front-runners for the All Blacks' No.10 jersey in the coming July series will square off at a packed Cake Tin in the Super Rugby Pacific final on Saturday night, with one final chance to convince Dave Rennie that they're the man for the job.
Rugby.body:
The two perceived front-runners for the All Blacks‘ No.10 jersey in the coming July series will square off at a packed Cake Tin in the Super Rugby Pacific final on Saturday night, with one final chance to convince Dave Rennie that they’re the man for the job.

Watching from the sidelines will be one-Test All Blacks playmaker Brett Cameron, having sustained a season-ending knee injury in round 2, just a year after missing 13 rounds of action due to another serious knee injury.

Rugby.title: Seven Wallabies, five All Blacks named in Super Rugby Pacific Team of the Year
Rugby.summary: Super Rugby Pacific has named its inaugural Team of the Year, headlined by Player of the Year (POTY) Quinn Tupaea and the man who lost the POTY tie-breaker, Timoci Tavatavanawai, in the midfield.
Rugby.body:
Super Rugby Pacific has named its inaugural Team of the Year, headlined by Player of the Year (POTY) Quinn Tupaea and the man who lost the POTY tie-breaker, Timoci Tavatavanawai, in the midfield.

There are eight New Zealand players on the team, including five All Blacks, and seven Australians, all of whom are capped Wallabies. The Western Force and Chiefs have two players in the form team, while the Reds and Highlanders have three, and the Hurricanes, Blues, Crusaders, Brumbies and Waratahs have one apiece.

Rugby.summary: The Series will see three regular season tournaments played across June and July 2026 in Kumagaya, Tochigi and Kitakyushu.
Rugby.body:
The 12th interaction of Taiyo Seimei Women’s Sevens Series is set to kick off this weekend, with a significant international flavour set to play out across the four tournaments in 2026.

The Series will see three regular season tournaments played across June and July 2026 in Kumagaya, Tochigi and Kitakyushu, with a grand final tournament set to wrap up the series in Sapporo on August 9th.

Rugby.title: 'I still don’t think I’m over it': Seuseu Naitoa Ah Kuoi on his finals heartbreaks
Rugby.summary: Seuseu Naitoa Ah Kuoi was as dazzled as the rest of New Zealand by the Chiefs' cathartic 49-12 demolition of the Crusaders in the Super Rugby Pacific semi-final on Friday night in Hamilton.
Rugby.body:
Seuseu Naitoa Ah Kuoi was as dazzled as the rest of New Zealand by the Chiefs’ cathartic 49-12 demolition of the Crusaders in the Super Rugby Pacific semi-final on Friday night in Hamilton. The Crusaders, who had beaten the Chiefs in the 2021, 2023 and 2025 finals, were banished in a record defeat.

“I was sitting on the bench for all 42 first-half points,” Ah Kuoi laughed with RugbyPass. “It was crazy. I was in awe. Nobody expects to beat the Crusaders by that margin. Where does the performance come from? It comes from preparation, trust and confidence. I could sense good energy from the boys, but man, that was something special.

Rugby.title: Why the renaissance man Billy Vunipola is Montpellier's inspiration
Rugby.summary: An England and Saracens legend, Billy Vunipola has had his ups and downs in his career but an Indian summer in Montpellier have seen him in his element
Rugby.body:
Two years ago this week was probably the low point in Montpellier’s modern history. The Mediterranean club had finished 13th in the Top 14 – just two years after winning the title – and were playing Grenoble for the right to remain in the elite. With three minutes remaining they were trailing 18-17 to the ProD2 club in what the French call the Barrage d’accession. Then they were awarded a penalty. Had Louis Carbonel missed Montpellier would have been relegated and perhaps imploded the way Biarritz and Beziers have in recent years.

Don’t miss it: France XV v England XV – live and free on RugbyPass TV & RugbyPass app. 17:15 BST, 19 June 2026. One man keeping a close eye on that match was Billy Vunipola. The Saracens and England No 8 had inked in a two-year deal to play for Montpellier, and he can’t have much fancied the idea of Thursday night rugby in towns he’d never heard of. With the greatest of respect, Nevers and Mont-de-Marsan are no Toulouse and Bordeaux.

Rugby.title: Worcester name Launchbury head of new academy
Rugby.summary: Worcester Warriors name former England lock Joe Launchbury as their new head of academy at Sixways.
Rugby.body:
Champ Rugby winners Worcester Warriors have appointed former England, Harlequins and Wasps forward Joe Launchbury as their new head of academy. The 35-year-old retired from playing at the end of the regular Prem season after three years with Quins and 51 appearances.

Launchbury won 70 caps for England, winning the Six Nations title three times and playing in two World Cups. The bulk of his playing career was spent with Wasps, where he made more than 150 appearances in two spells.

Rugby.title: Joe Launchbury remains in rugby post-retirement with eye-catching new role
Rugby.summary: A few weeks after his stellar playing career officially came to an end, ex-England lock Joe Launchbury has been unveiled as the head of Worcester Warriors' new academy.
Rugby.body:
A few weeks after his stellar playing career officially came to an end, ex-England lock Joe Launchbury has been unveiled as the head of Worcester Warriors’ new academy. Warriors’ head coach Matt Everard, who knows Launchbury from their days together at Wasps, is delighted to have the 35-year-old on board.

“Joe is one of the best leaders I’ve worked with. He has added to every environment he’s been in and embodies high performance,” he said. “With his playing experience combined with his academy coaching experience at Wasps & Harlequins, he is a hugely exciting addition to the club.”

A few weeks after his stellar playing career officially came to an end, ex-England lock Joe Launchbury has been unveiled as the head of Worcester Warriors’ new academy. Warriors’ head coach Matt Everard, who knows Launchbury from their days together at Wasps, is delighted to have the 35-year-old on board.

“Joe is one of the best leaders I’ve worked with. He has added to every environment he’s been in and embodies high performance,” he said. “With his playing experience combined with his academy coaching experience at Wasps & Harlequins, he is a hugely exciting addition to the club.”

Rugby.title: Lower tackle height locked in for grassroots game
Rugby.summary: Tackling above the base of the sternum will be outlawed in the grassroots game around the world by World Rugby from July.
Rugby.body:
Tackling above the base of the sternum will be outlawed in the grassroots game globally after World Rugby made a lower point of contact a permanent part of its law book. National unions will have the choice of setting either the base of the sternum or the waist as the upper limit for tackles.

The law comes into force at the start of July. It will mean little change for community rugby in England, Wales, Scotland and Ireland, all of which have been part of a multi-year trial of the new laws.

Rugby.title: 'Absolute animal at the minute': Henry Pollock on England No.8 rival
Rugby.summary: Henry Pollock has hailed Exeter Chiefs No.8 Greg Fisilau as an "absolute animal" ahead of their Gallagher PREM final showdown on Saturday, with the pair also competing for the England No.8 jersey.
Rugby.body:
Henry Pollock has hailed Exeter Chiefs No.8 Greg Fisilau as an “absolute animal” ahead of their Gallagher PREM final showdown on Saturday, with the pair also competing for the England No.8 jersey.

Both No.8s were instrumental in their semi-final victories over the weekend, with Pollock registering 23 carries as Saints beat Leicester Tigers 45-31, and Fisilau producing a staggering 29 tackles, 15 carries and a try in Exeter’s 26-27 comeback win over Bath.

Rugby.title: England to clear Feyi-Waboso and Mitchell for Prem final
Rugby.summary: Exeter wing Immanuel Feyi-Waboso and Northampton scrum-half Alex Mitchell are set to make surprise returns to their club sides for Saturday's Prem final at Twickenham's Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Exeter wing Immanuel Feyi-Waboso and Northampton scrum-half Alex Mitchell are set to make surprise returns to their club sides for Saturday's Prem final at Twickenham's Allianz Stadium.

Feyi-Waboso is recovering from surgery for a facial fracture sustained in Chiefs' win over Leicester at the end of May, while Mitchell strained his hamstring while taking part in an England training camp the previous week.

Rugby.title: Andy Farrell names three uncapped players in 36-man Ireland squad
Rugby.summary: Uncapped Connacht trio Billy Bohan, Sam Illo and Sean Jansen have been named in Andy Farrell's 36-man Ireland squad for their Nations Championship campaign in July.
Rugby.body:
Uncapped Connacht trio Billy Bohan, Sam Illo and Sean Jansen have been named in Andy Farrell’s 36-man Ireland squad for their Nations Championship campaign in July. The 20-year-old loosehead Bohan, who was called into the squad during the Guinness Six Nations, has found himself in the squad after Andrew Porter was ruled out of the three matches against Australia, Japan and the All Blacks.

Porter is not the only headline absentee in the squad, with Jack Crowley also ruled out after missing the tail end of Munster’s season with a leg injury. Sam Prendergast and Ciaran Frawley are the two playmaking options in the squad.

Rugby.summary: Uncapped Connacht trio Billy Bohan, Sam Illo and Sean Jansen are named in Ireland's squad for next month's Nations Championship fixtures, but injured fly-half Jack Crowley is ruled out.
Rugby.body:
Uncapped Connacht trio Billy Bohan, Sam Illo and Sean Jansen have been named in Ireland's squad for next month's Nations Championship fixtures, but injured fly-half Jack Crowley has been ruled out.

Crowley, who re-established himself as head coach Andy Farrell's first-choice out-half during the Six Nations, has not been included after Munster boss Clayton McMillan revealed he had been diagnosed with a blood vessel issue.

Rugby.summary: United Rugby Championship, Champions League and EPCR Challenge Cup broadcast partner Premier Sports is to show every game of the upcoming World Rugby Junior World Championship live.
Rugby.body:
United Rugby Championship, Champions League and EPCR Challenge Cup broadcast partner Premier Sports is to show every game of the upcoming World Rugby Junior World Championship live.

The deal comes hot on the heels of Premier Sports’ first-ever involvement in the Guinness Men’s Six Nations, when five of the 15 matches were streamed, as a further commitment to international rugby.

Rugby.title: New tackle height among five law changes confirmed by World Rugby
Rugby.summary: World Rugby have approved a permanent reduction in the legal tackle height in community rugby following successful trials involving more than 150,000 tackles across 10 unions over the past two years.
Rugby.body:
World Rugby have approved a permanent reduction in the legal tackle height in community rugby following successful trials involving more than 150,000 tackles across 10 unions over the past two years.

The law, which comes into force from July 1 for seasons beginning after that date, will allow unions to choose a legal tackle height of either the waist or the base of the sternum. The decision was ratified by the World Rugby Council in Dublin.

Rugby.title: Long and Turner-Hall take full-time roles at Quins
Rugby.summary: Harlequins add rugby league legend Sean Long and ex-player Jordan Turner-Hall to full-time roles on their backroom staff.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins have added Great Britain rugby league legend Sean Long and former player Jordan Turner-Hall to full-time roles on their first-team backroom staff. Long, who was previously on the Quins staff in the 2019-20 season and acted as a part-time consultant this term, has been promoted to the full-time role of skills and transition coach.

Turner-Hall, who has been with the club for 24 years, first as a player and then as an academy coach, has been named defence and transition coach. They will work under head coach Jason Gilmore as Quins aim to shake off a malaise that saw them finish ninth in the Prem table this season, their worst performance in eight years.

Rugby.title: Ospreys' Aussie recruitment drive continues with fly-half signed up
Rugby.summary: The Ospreys have confirmed their fourth new addition for the 26/27 season and their third signing with Australian connections, with Lawson Creighton set to join the club from the NSW Waratahs.
Rugby.body:
The Ospreys have confirmed their fourth new addition for the 26/27 season and their third signing with Australian connections, with Lawson Creighton set to join the club from the NSW Waratahs.

Creighton began his Super Rugby career with the Queensland Reds, making his debut in 2021, before transferring to the Waratahs four years later. The 6’1 tall fly-half, who can also play centre and full-back, made 26 appearances for the ‘Tahs and will provide cover for Wales international Dan Edwards.

Rugby.title: Nick Evans lands new job alongside former All Black
Rugby.summary: Harlequins legend Nick Evans is heading to coach in Japan next season following his departure from the Twickenham Stoop and has agreed a deal to become an assistant coach with Toshiba Brave Lupus.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins legend Nick Evans is heading to coach in Japan next season following his departure from the Twickenham Stoop and has agreed a deal to become an assistant coach with Toshiba Brave Lupus.

The Brave Lupus, who play in Japan Rugby League One’s Division One, were beaten in the quarter-finals of this season’s competition 26-3 by Kubota Spears, who themselves lost the final 22-13 to Kobe Steel earlier this month.

Rugby.title: Where are they now? The sides when Northampton and Exeter last met at Twickenham
Rugby.summary: Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs will go head-to-head at Twickenham in the Gallagher PREM Final on Saturday for the right to be called champions of England, but it’s not the first time they have met in a final in South West London.
Rugby.body:
Northampton Saints and Exeter Chiefs will go head-to-head at Twickenham in the Gallagher PREM Final on Saturday for the right to be called champions of England, but it’s not the first time they have met in a final in South West London.

In the first eight years of the new millennium, the Chiefs played in the EDF Energy National Trophy Final four times and lost all four to Rotherham in 2002, Orrell in 2003, Cornish Pirates in 2007, and Saints 24-13 the following season.

Rugby.title: Bulls select Bok-heavy team to face Leinster in URC final
Rugby.summary: Willie le Roux and Francois Klopper will each play their 50th BKT URC match for the competition's leading South African team.
Rugby.body:
The Vodacom Bulls have selected 11 Springbok internationals in their starting XV, as they look to put past final disappointments behind them and be crowned champions of the URC.

With opponents Leinster stacked with Ireland players, Friday night’s showpiece match at Croke Park will be like a Test match in all but name. Head coach Johan Ackermann has stuck with the same matchday 23 that shocked Glasgow in the semi-finals with a 22-21 comeback win at Murrayfield a fortnight ago in the hope that they can finally get their hopes on the trophy.

Rugby.title: Leinster make three changes for Bulls URC final
Rugby.summary: Leinster captain Caelan Doris is fit to start Friday's United Rugby Championship final against the Bulls as Tadhg Furlong returns to the line-up.
Rugby.body:
Leinster captain Caelan Doris has been passed fit to start Friday's United Rugby Championship final against the Pretoria Bulls as Tadhg Furlong returns to the line-up. Ireland skipper Doris was a doubt after picking up a knee injury in the semi-final win over the Stormers but he will lead a side showing three changes for the Croke Park decider (19:30 BST).

Fit-again Tommy O'Brien returns on the right wing with James Lowe starting on the opposite side in his final game before departing the province. Hugo Keenan remains at full-back with Rieko Ioane and Jamie Osborne continuing in midfield, while Jamison Gibson-Park and Sam Prendergast are retained in the half-backs.

Rugby.title: Lowe starts in farewell as Leinster get double injury boost for URC final
Rugby.summary: Ireland and Leinster captain Caelan Doris has been passed fit to start in their United Rugby Championship final against the Bulls on Friday at Croke Park.
Rugby.body:
Ireland and Leinster captain Caelan Doris has been passed fit to start in their United Rugby Championship final against the Bulls on Friday at Croke Park. The No.8 was an injury doubt heading into the weekend after sustaining a knee injury in the semi-final victory over the Stormers, and was listed as a player that required further assessment earlier in the week alongside Dan Sheehan, who missed the semi-final with knee and shoulder injuries.

The pair are fit to play in the final, though Sheehan will start on the bench with Ronan Kelleher starting in the front-row. In Andrew Porter’s absence, Jerry Cahir will start at loosehead in his last Leinster appearance before joining Connacht, with Alex Usanov serving as his back-up on the bench.

Rugby.title: Quesada announces Italy squad and explains Ange Capuozzo's absence
Rugby.summary: Italy head coach Gonzalo Quesada has named his 33-man squad for this July's Nations Championship, with three uncapped players included and Ange Capuozzo notably absent.
Rugby.body:
Italy head coach Gonzalo Quesada has named his 33-man squad for this July’s Nations Championship, with three uncapped players included and Ange Capuozzo notably absent. Benetton back-row Giulio Marini, Zebre lock Alessandro Ortombina and Zebre centre Malik Faissal have all received their first senior call-ups, while prop Ion Neculai returns to the Azzurri set-up for the first time since 2022.

Italy open their campaign against Japan in Tokyo on July 4 before daunting clashes with New Zealand in Wellington and Australia in Perth as part of the inaugural Nations Championship.

Rugby.summary: Harlequins have continued their coaching overhaul ahead of the 2026/27 season by confirming new roles for former England centre Jordan Turner-Hall and rugby league great Sean Long.
Rugby.body:
Harlequins have continued their coaching overhaul ahead of the 2026/27 season by confirming new roles for former England centre Jordan Turner-Hall and rugby league great Sean Long.

Turner-Hall has been appointed defence and transition coach, extending his 24-year association with the club. The former Quins academy graduate, who earned two caps for England, made 181 appearances during his playing career and was part of the sides that won the Challenge Cup in 2011 and Premiership title in 2012.

Rugby.title: Ojomoh and Caluori to start for England against France XV
Rugby.summary: Max Ojomoh and Noah Caluori will have the chance to press their case for Test starts after being named in the England team to face a France XV on Friday.
Rugby.body:
Max Ojomoh and Noah Caluori will have the chance to press their case for Test starts after being named in the England backline to take on a France XV in a non-cap match in Vannes on Friday.

Centre Ojomoh scored a try and was named player of the match in the win over Argentina in November, but has not played for his country since. Nineteen-year-old Caluori, who has spelled out his ambition to make his England debut this summer, topped the Prem's regular-season try-scoring charts and dominated opponents aerially in a sensational breakout season with Saracens.

Rugby.title: New faces but same Saints DNA
Rugby.summary: Northampton Saints winger Tommy Freeman says the club has the same DNA as their 2024 winning squad despite a much-changed line-up.
Rugby.body:
Two years after winning their second top-flight title, Northampton Saints will return to Twickenham for a shot at a third - though for much of their squad, Saturday's Prem final will be a first.

Of the 23 players in Saints' matchday squad for their thrilling semi-final victory over Leicester Tigers, 13 did not feature in the 2024 final against Bath. Former skipper Courtney Lawes is among eight who played in that final to have exited the club since.

Tennis.title: Tim Henman Backs Emma Raducanu to Win Another Grand Slam: 'She Can Do It Again' - Tennis Temple
Tennis.summary: Tim Henman Backs Emma Raducanu to Win Another Grand Slam: 'She Can Do It Again' Tennis Temple - Live tennis
Tennis.body:
With Wimbledon approaching (June 29 to July 12), excitement is once again building around Emma Raducanu in the UK. Since her unexpected breakthrough in the summer of 2021, capped by her US Open triumph, the Briton has carried the hopes of a nation yearning for a local champion to win Wimbledon—a feat not achieved by a British woman since Virginia Wade in 1977.

Indeed, expectations are high again after her impressive run at the Queen's tournament. Returning to competition after several weeks off due to a virus, the 23-year-old showed encouraging form by reaching the final.

Tennis.title: Anisimova plots Wimbledon sequel with happier ending
Tennis.summary: Many wondered how long it would take Amanda Anisimova to recover from last year's Wimbledon final. About six weeks was the answer.
Tennis.body:
Amanda Anisimova will arrive at Wimbledon this year with plenty of good memories - but also in the hope she can "rewrite the story" with a much happier ending. Twelve months ago, Anisimova won a compelling two-and-a-half hour tussle in the semi-finals with world number one Aryna Sabalenka to reach a first Grand Slam final.

But just two days later she failed to win a single game in the showpiece against Iga Swiatek, losing 6-0 6-0 in only 57 minutes - a scoreline that had not been seen in a Wimbledon ladies' singles final since 1911.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu launches eco-friendly vegan deodorant ‘inspired by Kent upbringing’
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu launches eco-friendly vegan deodorant ‘inspired by Kent upbringing’ The Telegraph
Tennis.body:
Emma Raducanu has launched an eco-friendly vegan deodorant which she says is “inspired” by her upbringing in Kent. Raducanu, who sparked talk of a pre-Wimbledon relationship last week after being spotted in a clinch with a PR expert, unveiled a range of personal hygiene products on Tuesday known as “The Garden of England” collection.

It has been created in conjunction with Wild, a cosmetics company that describes itself as “the UK’s No 1 refillable bathroom brand”. The company aims to reduce plastic waste and also boasts of being vegan.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu would lose Wimbledon seeding amid controversial rule change proposal
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu would lose Wimbledon seeding amid controversial rule change proposal talkSPORT
Tennis.body:
A shake-up to the seedings at Wimbledon has been proposed that would see the number dramatically slashed in half. The idea, proposed by British tennis stalwarts John Lloyd and Andrew Castle, would see the number of seeds at Wimbledon go from 32 to just 16.

Had that been in place in 2026 then British number one Emma Raducanu would have felt the effects. The 23-year-old is set to be seeded at No32 at this year's tournament, which begins on June 29.

Tennis.title: GB's Adeshina two wins away from Wimbledon dream
Tennis.summary: Britain's Esther Adeshina beats Japan's Moyuka Uchijima for the biggest win of her career to move within two victories of a spot in the main draw at Wimbledon.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Esther Adeshina said it was a "dream" to be playing in Wimbledon qualifying after achieving the biggest win of her career to move within two victories of a place in the main draw.

The 24-year-old beat Japan's former top-50 player Moyuka Uchijima 6-1 3-6 7-5 in after almost two hours in stifling heat in Roehampton. World number 514 Adeshina, will next face France's Harmony Tan, who famously beat Serena Williams on her way to the Wimbledon fourth round in 2022.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper and Axel Arigato’s partnership makes tennis more stylish than ever
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper and Axel Arigato’s partnership makes tennis more stylish than ever Culted
Tennis.body:
For years, Jack Draper was known primarily as British tennis’ next great hope. As one of the brightest prospects to emerge from British tennis in the post-Andy Murray era, Draper’s journey has often been measured through rankings, tournament runs and the constant pressure that comes with carrying a nation’s hopes. Yet, as his game has matured, so too has the person behind it.

Today, Draper occupies a rare space in modern sport. He’s one of the most exciting players on the ATP Tour, while he’s also part of a generation stepping into different mediums outside of their sport. Of course, performance is always the priority (especially with Wimbledon around the corner) but personality, culture and self-expression have become increasingly important parts of the equation.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper Reflect On 'Inspirations' From Andy Murray After Successful ATP Return In Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper Reflect On 'Inspirations' From Andy Murray After Successful ATP Return In Eastbourne ubitennis.net
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper spoke about how inspirational Andy Murray has been as he made a successful start to their partnership. The three-time Grand Slam champion is now aiding Draper’s comeback as the Brit played his first match in two months on Monday.

It was a winning return for Draper who overcame Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 and is into the second round in Eastbourne. The Brit will now face compatriot and good friend Jack Pinnington Jones after Pinnington Jones saved match point to defeat Marco Trungelliti.

Tennis.title: How is UK heatwave impacting sport for players and fans?
Tennis.summary: BBC Sport explores the impact of the UK heatwave on sports events across the country
Tennis.body:
An unprecedented heatwave is beginning in the UK, with the June temperature record almost certainly set to be broken over the next few days. The current record high of 35.6C, set in 1976, is set to be surpassed as meteorologists forecast temperatures as high as 38C and 39C in some parts of the country.

The Met Office has issued a rare red warning for extreme heat for sections of England and Wales from 09:00 BST on Wednesday until 21:00 on Thursday. The red warning area includes London, Cardiff, the south west and south east, the Midlands, and the east of England.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu, global ambassador for Wild, launches her own scent with the brand
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu, global ambassador for Wild, launches her own scent with the brand Retail Times
Tennis.body:
Refillable personal care brand Wild and its Global Brand Ambassador, Emma Raducanu, have teamed up to create her very own scent, Garden of England. Inspired by her upbringing in Kent, Garden of England blends crisp rhubarb, fresh green leaves and rose petals, capturing the feeling of a British summer in the countryside.

The launch marks the latest milestone in Emma’s partnership with Wild, which began in 2025. As an athlete, Emma puts Wild through its paces every day, demonstrating that high-performance protection and natural formulas can go hand in hand.

Tennis.title: Everything to know about Wimbledon's main warm-up event and if it's worth going
Tennis.summary: Everything to know about Wimbledon's main warm-up event and if it's worth going North Wales Chronicle
Tennis.body:
I got the chance to spend a day at the main warm-up tournament to Wimbledon, but is it worth going? Wimbledon is a huge event in the tennis calendar, with it being one of the four main Grand Slam events along with the Australian Open, French Open and US Open.

As part of the build-up to the tournament, players will take part in a few smaller competitions. One of these is The Queen's Club Championships, which is held at the Queen's Club in West Kensington, London.

Tennis.title: Battling egos and stereotypes
Tennis.summary: In a sport like tennis, which prides itself on gender equality, why are there still so few female coaches and what is being done to address the issue?
Tennis.body:
The coaching box is one of the most visible places in tennis. Players often glance towards it after every point. Television cameras pan to it dozens of times during a match. Commentators spend time trying to dissect the reactions of those within it.

But if you look closely, whether at regular tour events or at Wimbledon in the coming fortnight, you will usually see most of the coaching staff inside it are men. Female coaches, even at the top of the women's game, are a rarity. Only four players inside the top 50 in singles have a woman as their primary coach.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper is turning Wimbledon's “energy” into a sneaker
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper is turning Wimbledon's “energy” into a sneaker British GQ
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper's ascent is difficult to ignore. At 24, the British tennis star has established himself as one of the biggest names on the ATP tour. And somewhere between all the training blocks and Grand Slam campaigns, he's become a bit of a sneaker designer as well.

The latest chapter of his ongoing collab with Axel Arigato is Rise, the second release in a three-part trilogy developed alongside the Swedish label. Following Dusk (which drew inspo from the clay courts of Roland-Garros), Rise turns its attention a lot closer to home.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu: ‘Learning On The Public Stage Has Been A Huge Challenge’
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu: ‘Learning On The Public Stage Has Been A Huge Challenge’ Grazia Daily UK
Tennis.body:
The first time I interviewed Emma Raducanu she was just 19. It was barely a year after she had blasted on to the world stage with her shock US Open win – the first British woman to win a major singles championship since 1977 – and her reservation at answering my questions was palpable. But today, sitting across from the now 23-year-old tennis star, she has a new energy. Relaxed and self-assured, she's come a long way from the teenager who appeared overwhelmed by the weight of everyone else's expectations.

'I didn't have the backbone [back then],' she says. 'I would try and justify a certain answer in my head because I thought that was the "right" thing to do or say or the "right" person to work with, even if it didn't feel right. People were telling me it was a good idea and I found it hard to remove myself from situations even if I knew it was wrong. Every mistake or decision was up for scrutiny, so you're learning on the go, but on a public stage. I wish I had trusted my instincts more. In the last few years, I've been more comfortable being open and honest.'

Tennis.title: The Andy Murray factor that helped injury-plagued Jack Draper get back to ‘full power’ at Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: The Andy Murray factor that helped injury-plagued Jack Draper get back to ‘full power’ at Eastbourne The Independent
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper says Andy Murray’s impact has fired him back to his “powerful” best after a triumphant return to competitive tennis at Eastbourne. The former World No 4, who has plummeted down the rankings after an injury-ridden few months, clinched a first-round victory over American Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 (7-5) in front of new coach Murray on the south coast.

Draper, a former US Open semi-finalist, cut a restless figure in Devonshire Park after a series of chronic arm and knee injuries had sidelined him since April. But the 24-year-old year dusted off the cobwebs in style to warm up for a Wimbledon return in perfect style and set up a last 16 date with fellow Brit Jack Pinnington Jones.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper hails new coach Andy Murray after winning return at Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper hails new coach Andy Murray after winning return at Eastbourne The Guardian
Tennis.body:
An amusing question at the beginning of this new, hopeful chapter in Jack Draper’s career was tendered to the 24-year-old without hesitation before his return to competition in Eastbourne this week. In the heat of a dispiriting, stressful battle on court, when things are going badly and he might need to discharge some of his tension, would he ever allow himself to scream at Andy Murray?

The prospect of hollering expletives at his idol, friend and new coach drew laughter from Draper. Then he shook his head: “I don’t think so,” Draper said. “Maybe a few times I’ve said something to my team but it’s mainly anger at myself. I’ve never been someone who goes off at my time, because I have a great relationship with all of them. I have too much respect for Andy and Trots [James Trotman, his other coach] to be doing that.”

Tennis.title: Jack Draper reveals one thing he refuses to do for Andy Murray as coaching link-up ahead of Wimbledon begins
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper reveals one thing he refuses to do for Andy Murray as coaching link-up ahead of Wimbledon begins talkSPORT
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper joked he would not be calling Sir Andy Murray by his proper title anytime soon. Murray, a two-time Wimbledon winner, has started coaching Draper for the grass court season.

The 24-year-old made a winning return to competitive action at Eastbourne by beating American Marcos Giron 6-4, 7-6 (7-5). It was his first time back in court in two months in an injury ravaged spell for the young Brit.

Tennis.title: Eastbourne Open: Britain's Jack Draper defeats Marcos Giron in first match under new coach Andy Murray
Tennis.summary: Eastbourne Open: Britain's Jack Draper defeats Marcos Giron in first match under new coach Andy Murray Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper beat lucky loser Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 (7-5) in an impressive straight sets first-round victory at the Eastbourne Open in his first game under new coach Andy Murray.

Draper has been hampered by injuries since the US Open last August, with a knee problem causing his latest stint on the sidelines since April. The former World No 4 returned on the grass of Eastbourne after only playing nine matches throughout 2026 one week before Wimbledon starts with British tennis great Murray as part of his coaching set-up for the first time in competition.

Tennis.title: Draper 'wins ugly like Murray' on injury return
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper says he drew inspiration from new coach Sir Andy Murray to claim an "ugly" win in his first competitive match in over two months.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Jack Draper says he drew inspiration from new coach Sir Andy Murray to claim an "ugly" win in his first competitive match in over two months. A knee injury has kept the 24-year-old on the sidelines since April, but he has been working with Murray in recent weeks after joining up with the three-time Grand Slam champion for the grass-court season.

The pair's partnership got off to a winning start at Eastbourne as Draper held off a late charge by American Marcos Giron to secure a 6-4 7-6 (7-5) victory. Asked what it was like having a knight in his coaching box, Draper joked: "I haven't called him 'Sir' yet - that's not going to happen.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper hails Sir Andy Murray for guiding him to ‘ugly win’ at Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper hails Sir Andy Murray for guiding him to ‘ugly win’ at Eastbourne Somerset County Gazette
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper credited new coach Sir Andy Murray with inspiring him to an “ugly” win on his latest injury comeback. Three-time grand-slam winner Murray watched from a court-side box on Monday as Draper beat American lucky loser Marcos Giron 6-4 7-6 (5) in the first round of the Lexus Eastbourne Open.

The former world number four has been plagued by chronic arm and knee issues during the past year but felt no ill effects during a solid display on a sweltering afternoon at Devonshire Park.

Tennis.title: Ex-Wimbledon champion Vondrousova given four-year ban
Tennis.summary: Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova is banned for four years for refusing an anti-doping test in December 2025.
Tennis.body:
Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been banned for four years for refusing an anti-doping test. Vondrousova, 26, was charged by the International Tennis Integrity Agency (ITIA) after denying a doping control officer entry to her home to conduct the test in December 2025.

The Czech player said in April she feared for her safety when the officer called and said they failed to follow "protocol". But a tribunal determined Vondrousova, who became the first unseeded player to win the Wimbledon women's singles title in 2023, provided "no compelling justification" for refusing a test.

Tennis.title: Draper returns at Eastbourne & Wimbledon qualifying begins
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper makes his return at Eastbourne and Wimbledon qualifying gets under way - follow it across the BBC.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Jack Draper makes his long-awaited return from injury at Eastbourne - and you can watch it live on the BBC. Former world number four Draper beat lucky loser Marcus Giron in his first match for two months, and will face fellow Briton Jack Pinnington Jones in the second round.

Draper is accompanied in Eastbourne by new coach Sir Andy Murray, who has joined the Briton's team for the grass court season. World number nine and four-time winner Taylor Fritz is the highest-ranked player in the draw, which also includes recent Queen's quarter-finalist Arthur Fery and highly-rated Brazilian teenager Joao Fonseca.

Britain's Jack Draper says he drew inspiration from new coach Sir Andy Murray to claim an "ugly" win in his first competitive match in over two months. A knee injury has kept the 24-year-old on the sidelines since April, but he has been working with Murray in recent weeks after joining up with the three-time Grand Slam champion for the grass-court season.

The pair's partnership got off to a winning start at Eastbourne as Draper held off a late charge by American Marcos Giron to secure a 6-4 7-6 (7-5) victory. Asked what it was like having a knight in his coaching box, Draper joked: "I haven't called him 'Sir' yet - that's not going to happen.

Tennis.title: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Jack Draper beats Marcos Giron on injury return in Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Lexus Eastbourne Open 2026: Jack Draper beats Marcos Giron on injury return in Eastbourne LTA
Tennis.body:
Former world No.4 Jack Draper enjoyed a winning return to action on the ATP Tour after securing a straight sets victory over lucky loser Marcos Giron at the Lexus Eastbourne Open.

Draper showed little signs of rustiness in his first round clash against the lucky loser, wrapping up the opening set in just 36 minutes before sealing an impressive 6-4, 7-6(5) victory.

Tennis.title: Evans not motivated more by Wimbledon wildcard omission
Tennis.summary: Dan Evans says missing out on a Wimbledon main-draw wildcard has not motivated him more in qualifying as he beat Juan Carlos Prado Angelo in the first round.
Tennis.body:
Dan Evans says missing out on a Wimbledon main-draw wildcard has not motivated him more in qualifying as he overcame Juan Carlos Prado Angelo in the first round. The 36-year-old, who will end his career after the championships, has been given a wildcard into the men's doubles with Henry Searle, who he has been working with since January.

However, Evans missed out on one of the eight singles wildcards, meaning he needs to win three matches in Roehampton this week to play one last singles at SW19. The former British number one expressed frustration earlier in June at not being given a wildcard for Queen's , saying it would have been a "classy gesture" to get one.

Tennis.title: Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova banned for four years for refusing anti-doping test last year
Tennis.summary: Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova banned for four years for refusing anti-doping test last year Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been suspended from tennis for four years for refusing an anti-doping test. An independent tribunal concluded there was "no compelling justification" for the 26-year-old Czech to have not submitted a sample when notified by a doping control officer, out of competition and at her home, on the night of December 3, 2025.

Vondrousova, the 2023 Wimbledon champion and 2020 Olympic Silver medallist, is not allowed to play in, coach at, or attend any events organised or sanctioned by the ITF, WTA, ATP, the Grand Slams, or any national association until 21 June 2030.

Tennis.title: Williams is the greatest
Tennis.summary: Serena Williams will return to the singles court at Wimbledon aged 44 - BBC Sport analyses if she is ready and the challenges she will face.
Tennis.body:
Every great fighter thinks they have one great fight left in them. That old adage is usually applied to boxers who come out of retirement for another swing - but it feels particularly relevant as 44-year-old Serena Williams makes the next step in her comeback to professional tennis.

The 23-time Grand Slam singles champion will return to the singles court at Wimbledon next week after a four-year absence from the sport she once dominated. In many eyes, the American superstar is the greatest player of all time - certainly in the women's game - and she clearly believes she has the ability to win matches again.

Tennis.title: In the foothills of the Dolomites, a champion was made
Tennis.summary: Jannik Sinner is the world's best tennis player. This is the journey that forged a champion.
Tennis.body:
It was in the foothills of the Dolomite mountains that a three-year-old Jannik Sinner took his first steps towards becoming the world's best player. It started on the ski slopes, where he was so good he later became a giant slalom runner-up in the junior national championships.

Sinner also showed enormous promise on the football pitch with an instinctive ability to play with both feet. But tennis soon started to take over Sinner's life in the mountain village of Sexten, just a short hike from the Austrian border.

Tennis.title: Serena Williams: American 23-time Grand Slam winner receives Wimbledon women's singles wild card
Tennis.summary: Serena Williams: American 23-time Grand Slam winner receives Wimbledon women's singles wild card Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Serena Williams has been handed the final Wimbledon singles wild card into the women's main draw as she prepares for her Grand Slam comeback. The seven-time Wimbledon singles champion was already assured of a return to the championships for the first time in four years after accepting a doubles wild card with sister Venus.

Now the 44-year-old American legend will make a stunning return to singles after being handed the final wild card by the All England Club on Sunday. "This is not a drill," the tournament wrote in a post on its Instagram account.

Tennis.title: Serena Williams to make singles comeback at Wimbledon
Tennis.summary: Serena Williams will make a surprise return to singles action at Wimbledon later this month after accepting a wildcard.
Tennis.body:
Serena Williams will make a surprise return to singles action at Wimbledon later this month after accepting a wildcard. The 44-year-old has been given the final spot in the women's main draw - filling a gap left following the initial announcement on Tuesday.

Williams has played two doubles matches since making her competitive return earlier this month after a four-year absence. She has won the Wimbledon singles title seven times but remains one short of Margaret Court's long-standing women's record of 24 Grand Slam wins.

Tennis.title: Injury struggles 'like watching your decline'
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper says he felt he was watching his own decline during his recent injury struggles - but the Briton does not picture his career "being all injuries".
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper says he felt he was watching his own decline during his recent injury struggles - but the Briton does not picture his career "being all injuries". The 24-year-old will make his competitive return to action at Eastbourne on Monday after struggling with injuries to his arm, knee and shoulder in the past year.

Draper's injury problems began at Wimbledon last year, when he was having the best run of his career. In an 18-month period, Draper reached a Grand Slam semi-final, reached a high of fourth in the world rankings and won his first ATP 1000 title at Indian Wells.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper interview: I feared I would never be the same again
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper interview: I feared I would never be the same again The Telegraph

Tennis.title: Jack Draper energised for return at Eastbourne with inspiration from coach Murray
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper energised for return at Eastbourne with inspiration from coach Murray The Guardian
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper believes his “very special” relationship with Andy Murray and the faith the Scot has in him will help him to find a way through the toughest period of his career as he returns to the circuit at Eastbourne after an injury-ravaged 11 months.

Draper is due to compete for the first time in two and a half months at the Eastbourne International on Monday after being sidelined for the vast majority of the past year due to chronic arm and knee injuries. As he tries to be ready for Wimbledon, which begins next a week on Monday, this will also be his first tournament since asking Murray, his childhood idol and a friend, to join his team as coach.

Tennis.title: Cerundolo wins biggest title of career at Queen's as parents fly in just in time
Tennis.summary: Francisco Cerundolo secures the biggest title of his career by fighting back to beat Tommy Paul in the longest men's singles final at Queen's.
Tennis.body:
Francisco Cerundolo had to fight through the longest Queen's final in history to claim the biggest title of his career, but he could hardly have timed it better. His parents arrived from their flight from Argentina in just enough time to see their son triumph - despite his father's fear of flying.

Cerundolo had never won a title above ATP 250 level before and it looked as though his wait would continue when he went a set and a break down against American Tommy Paul. But after an enthralling three hours and two minutes on court, a triumphant Cerundolo threw himself to the floor in celebration after a 6-7 (4-7) 6-4 6-3 victory.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper boosted by coach Andy Murray ahead of Wimbledon: 'He believes in me and my tennis'
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper boosted by coach Andy Murray ahead of Wimbledon: 'He believes in me and my tennis' Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
"He believes in me and my tennis" - Jack Draper has revealed he has received a big boost from his new coach Andy Murray ahead of Wimbledon. The former British No 1 prepares to return to tennis action at the Eastbourne Open on Monday when he tackles American Brandon Nakashima, who made it as far as the semi-finals at Queen's before losing to eventual champion Francisco Cerundolo.

Draper has been hampered by injuries since the US Open last August, with a knee problem causing his latest stint on the sidelines since April. But he returns on the grass of Eastbourne one week before Wimbledon starts with Murray, a two-time champion at the All England Club, as part of his coaching set-up for the first time in competition.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper ready to rebuild after year spent ‘watching his decline’
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper ready to rebuild after year spent ‘watching his decline’ The Independent
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper felt he was watching his own decline during a year-long injury nightmare as he bids to put his career back on track under the guidance of Andy Murray. The former world number four has plummeted to 113 in the rankings after being plagued by chronic arm and knee issues, in addition to a shoulder problem.

Draper has enlisted three-time grand slam champion Murray as his coach for the grass-court season as he targets a sustained return to tour-level competition. With Wimbledon set to start next week, he is due to play his first match in more than two months on Monday when he faces American world number 32 Brandon Nakashima in the first round at Eastbourne.

Tennis.title: Queen's Club: Francisco Cerundolo beats Tommy Paul to win tournament in longest-ever final at HSBC Championships
Tennis.summary: Queen's Club: Francisco Cerundolo beats Tommy Paul to win tournament in longest-ever final at HSBC Championships Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Francisco Cerundolo was crowned Queen's champion after beating Tommy Paul 6-7 (4-7) 6-4 6-3 in the longest final in championship history at three hours and three minutes. Paul, the 2024 champion who was on a nine-match winning streak in the tournament having missed last year's edition through injury, battled back from 3-5 down to win a tie-break 7-4 in a 66-minute first set.

Cerundolo, though, responded by fighting off a vital break point at 4-4 to then break himself and force a decider. - Not got Sky? Get Sky Sports or stream with no contract The No 8 seed then seized the momentum to surge 5-2 ahead in the third set, but squandered three match points against the serve as Paul fought to stay in the match.

Tennis.title: Bouzkova beats Navarro to clinch Nottingham title
Tennis.summary: Marie Bouzkova overcomes an ankle injury to beat Emma Navarro and win the Nottingham Open for her first grass-court singles title.
Tennis.body:
Marie Bouzkova overcame an ankle injury to beat Emma Navarro and win the Nottingham Open for her first grass-court singles title. Czech fourth seed Bouzkova twice received treatment on the injury but rallied to see off the American 7-6 (7-5) 4-6 6-2.

It is a second title of the year for the 27-year-old after she won the clay-court event in Bogota in April. Bouzkova twice led Navarro by a break in the first set but was reeled in by the former world number eight.

Tennis.title: How Murray was tempted back to tennis
Tennis.summary: British tennis icon Andy Murray discusses returning to coaching with Jack Draper, Serena Williams' comeback and filling the void of not playing.
Tennis.body:
Andy Murray has been in the Centre Court coaching box once before. Murray famously clambered up to the stands - using the canvas roof of a commentary booth as a leg up - to celebrate with his team in 2013 after ending Britain's 77-year wait for a Wimbledon men's champion.

Next week, Murray could be back in those seats shouting down instructions to fellow Briton Jack Draper, having joined his coaching team for the grass-court season. Two years on from his retirement, Murray sat down with BBC Sport for an exclusive interview about what tempted him back to tennis, why he was not surprised by Serena Williams' comeback and how he is filling the void left by not playing.

Next week, Murray could be back in those seats shouting down instructions to fellow Briton Jack Draper, having joined his coaching team for the grass-court season. Two years on from his retirement, Murray sat down with BBC Sport for an exclusive interview about what tempted him back to tennis, why he was not surprised by Serena Williams' comeback and how he is filling the void left by not playing.

Next week, Murray could be back in those seats shouting down instructions to fellow Briton Jack Draper, having joined his coaching team for the grass-court season. Two years on from his retirement, Murray sat down with BBC Sport for an exclusive interview about what tempted him back to tennis, why he was not surprised by Serena Williams' comeback and how he is filling the void left by not playing.

Tennis.title: Sell-out crowds and joy: how Queen’s Club women’s tournament outshone the men | Tumaini Carayol
Tennis.summary: Sell-out crowds and joy: how Queen’s Club women’s tournament outshone the men | Tumaini Carayol The Guardian
Tennis.body:
One of the more amusing sights at the Queen’s Club tournament each year comes before even entering the grounds. On the first day of play on Monday, a deluge of spectators invariably descend on Barons Court station, just 150 metres from the entrance.

So many people passing through a tiny London Underground station naturally means long queues at the barriers. That congestion is not helped by many of them comically pausing in front of the gates to frantically search for their debit cards or desperately try to unlock their phones.

Tennis.title: Comeback opponent backs Jack Draper ahead of Eastbourne
Tennis.summary: Comeback opponent backs Jack Draper ahead of Eastbourne The Argus
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper has been backed to return to the top of men’s tennis by the player he will begin his comeback against next week. Draper has not played a match since retiring against Tomas Etcheverry in Barcelona in mid-April with a knee problem.

That forced the 24-year-old to miss the French Open, and a planned return to action at Queen’s Club this week was also shelved. Draper’s almost year-long injury problems have seen his world ranking plummet from four to 113.

Tennis.title: Fritz beats Zverev again to reach Halle final
Tennis.summary: Taylor Fritz's impressive form on grass continues as he beats French Open champion Alexander Zverev to reach the final in Halle.
Tennis.body:
Taylor Fritz's impressive form on the grass courts continued as he fought back to beat French Open champion Alexander Zverev and reach the final in Halle. Germany's Zverev won his first major singles title at Roland Garros earlier this month and was on an 10 match winning streak, but struggled physically in scorching hot conditions and needed medical treatment throughout the match.

Fritz remained steady to rally from a set down to beat his hampered opponent 6-7 (4-7) 6-4 7-5 and reach a second successive grass-court final. The American backed up his quarter-final win over third seed Ben Shelton - who beat him in the Stuttgart final last week - with another top five victory, while also extending his winning run against world number three Zverev to seven matches.

Tennis.title: ‘Andy is incredible’: Jack Draper credits Murray ahead of Eastbourne return - Tennis Temple
Tennis.summary: ‘Andy is incredible’: Jack Draper credits Murray ahead of Eastbourne return Tennis Temple - Live tennis
Tennis.body:
After more than two months away, Jack Draper will return to competition next week at the ATP 250 event in Eastbourne. In an interview released by the British Lawn Tennis Association this Saturday, the former world No.5 expressed his joy at playing again on a tournament he particularly enjoys.

“I feel good. I’m very excited to come back to a tournament, especially here at Eastbourne, because I haven’t played here for about four years. I’m very happy to be back. This is where I first broke into the top 100. I remember playing Brooksby in the first round, then beating Schwartzman before losing to Cressy.”

Tennis.title: Aryna Sabalenka: Top seed stunned by Jessica Pegula in deciding set as American reaches Berlin Open final
Tennis.summary: Aryna Sabalenka: Top seed stunned by Jessica Pegula in deciding set as American reaches Berlin Open final Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Top seed Aryna Sabalenka saw her Wimbledon preparations take a major hit as she was stunned by Jessica Pegula in the deciding set of the Berlin Open semi-finals. The American sealed her fourth win over Sabalenka in 13 WTA Tour meetings with a stunning 6-4 6-7 (4-7) 6-0 victory as she secured a spot in the final.

The 28-year-old, who exited the French Open in the quarter-finals to Russian Diana Shnaider, struggled to find her rhythm after a slow start, and Pegula took the opening set following an early break.

Tennis.title: HSBC Championships 2026: Tommy Paul through to final with win over Ugo Humbert
Tennis.summary: HSBC Championships 2026: Tommy Paul through to final with win over Ugo Humbert LTA
Tennis.body:
Tommy Paul is playing like a man on a mission at this year’s HSBC Championships, with the American now set to compete in the Queen’s final once again. The 2024 champion was in devastating form to sail past France’s Ugo Humbert and reach his fourth ATP final of the season already.

Paul managed to soak up the aggressive, flat groundstrokes to defeat the world No.33 6-3, 6-3 in just an hour and 19 minutes. He is now on a nine-match winning streak at The Queen’s Club, having missed last year with injury, and has been relishing the chance to get back on the grass.

Tennis.title: ‘I’ve Earned The Right To Be Back’ – Jack Draper Relishing Return With New Coach Murray
Tennis.summary: ‘I’ve Earned The Right To Be Back’ – Jack Draper Relishing Return With New Coach Murray ubitennis.net
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper has vowed to give his all when he returns to action next week at the Eastbourne International following months of injury setbacks. Draper, who has been ranked as high as fourth in the world, has played only three tournaments this season, and in one of them, he was forced to retire from his opening match. Since April, he has been sidelined from action due to a knee tendon injury. That setback came after he missed nearly six months of the Tour between 2025 and 2026 due to bone bruising in his left serving arm.

“I’ve been injured for a long time. I’ve had a lot of setbacks, a lot of struggles, especially in this last year,” Draper said during an interview with the LTA. “But I’ve kept on going. I’ve kept on working really hard. I’m just pumped to be back at a tournament.

Tennis.title: Sabalenka loses another deciding set 6-0 as Pegula triumphs
Tennis.summary: Aryna Sabalenka loses another deciding set without winning a game as Jessica Pegula beats the world number one to reach the Berlin Open final.
Tennis.body:
Aryna Sabalenka lost another deciding set without winning a game as an impressive Jessica Pegula beat the world number one to reach the Berlin Open final. Belarusian Sabalenka never looked settled against Pegula, who recovered from losing a tight second set to come through 6-4 6-7 (4-7) 6-0.

American Pegula had lost five of her past six matches against Sabalenka but her flat shots and superb return of serve anchored her performance this time around. For Sabalenka it is another disappointing deciding-set performance, having lost the third set of her French Open quarter-final 6-0 after squandering a set and double break lead.

He subsequently withdrew from Queen’s, but will be back fit in time for Eastbourne, the week before Wimbledon. Who is your early shout to win the men’s Wimbledon title? Unfortunately for Draper, the draw hasn’t done him any favours.

Tennis.title: Draper returns but Eastbourne draw delivers immediate danger
Tennis.summary: Draper returns but Eastbourne draw delivers immediate danger ATP Tour
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper will face Brandon Nakashima in his return to action next week at the Lexus Eastbourne Open following Saturday’s draw. The British lefty has not competed since Barcelona in April but will take to court at the ATP 250 event in Eastbourne and will have Andy Murray courtside. The ATP No. 1 Club Member Murray is coaching Draper during the grass-court season.

Draper – making his second appearance in Eastbourne after a 2022 semi-final run – and Nakashima are both in the bottom half of the draw alongside second seed Joao Fonseca, who will start against a qualifier. Nakashima is in semi-final action at The Queen’s Club on Saturday, having upset top seed Alex de Minaur in the last eight. The American has started working with Wayne Ferreira this week, which you can read more about here.

Tennis.title: Emma Raducanu’s latest decision looks confusing right before Wimbledon
Tennis.summary: Emma Raducanu’s latest decision looks confusing right before Wimbledon Tennishead
Tennis.body:
After her run at Queen’s, Emma Raducanu should head to Wimbledon brimming with confidence. After all, she excelled on the grass once more, recapturing that aggressive, front-foot brand of tennis which once earned her huge success.

In the years since that 2021 US Open triumph, the British number one has become confused in her methods whilst also suffering from relentless injury and health concerns. It feels like only now that Raducanu has re-hired Andrew Richardson, the man who led her to a maiden major title, that things are getting back on track.

Tennis.title: 'Now I know how it feels to play me'
Tennis.summary: Aryna Sabalenka says she now knows "how it feels to play against me" as she battled past Nikola Bartunkova to reach the Berlin Open semi-finals.
Tennis.body:
Aryna Sabalenka says she now knows "how it feels to play against me" as she claimed a much-needed comeback win over Nikola Bartunkova to reach the Berlin Open semi-finals. World number one Sabalenka found herself a set and 4-0 down after being overpowered in the early stages by talented Czech Bartunkova.

The Belarusian was error-prone, irritable and anonymous on serve in a performance reminiscent of her surprise French Open quarter-final loss. However, Sabalenka steadied herself and refound her rhythm to come through 2-6 7-6 (7-2) 6-4.

Tennis.title: HSBC Championships 2026: Tommy Paul knocks out Alejandro Davidovich Fokina to keep win streak alive
Tennis.summary: HSBC Championships 2026: Tommy Paul knocks out Alejandro Davidovich Fokina to keep win streak alive LTA
Tennis.body:
Tommy Paul will now be a firm favourite for the HSBC Championships title heading into the semi-finals Saturday at The Queen’s Club. Former world No.8 Paul knocked out fourth seed Alejandro Davidovich Fokina 6-3, 7-6(4) in an hour and 29 minutes to reach the final four.

The 2024 champion is now on an eight-match winning streak at the HSBC Championships, having missed last year’s tournament due to injury. This will be Paul’s fifth ATP semi-final of the season as he aims to seal his second title of 2026 – adding to a trophy-winning campaign in Houston.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper set for first match since joining forces with Andy Murray
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper set for first match since joining forces with Andy Murray The Independent
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper will make his return from injury at the Lexus Eastbourne Open next week. The former world number four has not played a match since retiring during the third set of a clash against Tomas Etcheverry in Barcelona in mid-April with a knee problem.

That forced him out of the French Open and his comeback has been delayed further by a shoulder issue, prompting fears he could miss Wimbledon. But Draper is now sufficiently recovered to take his place in the draw for the warm-up tournament, which will mark the first time he has new coach Andy Murray in his corner.

Tennis.title: Fery's Queen's run ended by Cerundolo in narrow loss
Tennis.summary: Britain's Arthur Fery narrowly misses out on another career milestone as he falls to Francisco Cerundolo in the Queen's quarter-finals.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Arthur Fery narrowly missed out on another career milestone as he fell to world number 27 Francisco Cerundolo in three tight sets in the Queen's quarter-finals. Fery, ranked 140th in the world, had already broken new ground by beating Adrian Mannarino to reach the last eight of an ATP Tour competition for the first time.

On Friday, he put himself on the brink of another step forward, battling back from a set and a break down against the Argentine on a sweltering day in London. A spot in the semi-finals was in Fery's grasp as he led by a break in the third set - but Cerundolo rediscovered his rhythm to come through 7-6 (7-1) 3-6 6-4 in two hours and 39 minutes.

Tennis.title: Draper to return at Eastbourne with new coach Murray
Tennis.summary: Britain's Jack Draper is set to make his return from injury at Eastbourne under new coach Andy Murray - but Emma Raducanu has opted not to take a wildcard.
Tennis.body:
Britain's Jack Draper will make his long-awaited return from injury at Eastbourne next week - and he will be watched by new coach Andy Murray. The 24-year-old has not played competitively since early April and needs match practice before Wimbledon starts on 29 June.

After withdrawing from Queen's last week, Draper now feels ready to play at Eastbourne and prove his fitness before the Championships begin. Eastbourne will be Draper's first tournament under the guidance of Murray, who was brought into the 2024 US Open semi-finalist's team last month.

Tennis.title: Corentin Moutet fined almost entire Queen's Club earnings after rude outburst
Tennis.summary: Corentin Moutet fined almost entire Queen's Club earnings after rude outburst The Sun
Tennis.body:
POTTY-MOUTHED Corentin Moutet has been fined £30,000 – more than 90 per cent of his Queen’s prize money – for his X-rated rant on live TV. Last Tuesday, childish Moutet, 27, said the word “f***” seven times while being interviewed by BBC reporter Jenny Drummond on the Andy Murray Arena.

This was immediately after his 6-7 6-4 7-6 win over big-serving fellow countryman Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard, 22, in round one. Prize money for reaching the Last 16 – where he was defeated 6-4 6-3 by Spaniard Alejandro Davidovich Fokina and marked the loss by smacking his racquet on the ground – should have earned him £33,000.

Tennis.title: Moutet loses almost all prize money for swearing on BBC TV
Tennis.summary: France's Corentin Moutet loses almost his entire Queen's prize money after being fined $40,000 for swearing seven times in a live BBC television interview.
Tennis.body:
France's Corentin Moutet has lost almost his entire Queen's prize money after being fined $40,000 (£30,325) for swearing seven times in a live BBC television interview. The 27-year-old has been given the maximum punishment for unsportsmanlike behaviour at the west London club by the ATP Tour - but has appealed against the fine.

Moutet earned prize money of about £32,800 for reaching the second round at the ATP 500 event. The world number 36 repeatedly swore in an on-court interview following his first-round win against compatriot Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard on Tuesday.

Tennis.title: 'Opened new world'
Tennis.summary: Abbie Breakwell reflects on the chance meeting that launched her wheelchair tennis career as she prepares to play at the venue where it happened.
Tennis.body:
Abbie Breakwell was a ball crew member darting around the tennis court at a tournament in Nottingham when a stranger from the stands gave her a life-changing idea. It was simply to pick up the racquet herself and play.

Breakwell lives with Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease - a muscle-wasting condition that means her legs have become weaker over time - as well as spinal syrinx, which is a ball of fluid on the spinal cord that she did not have diagnosed until she was 13.

Tennis.title: GB's Klugman beaten in Nottingham second round
Tennis.summary: British teenager Hannah Klugman says she feels "very different" after a week practising with "some great players" following her second-round Nottingham Open defeat.
Tennis.body:
British teenager Hannah Klugman says she feels "very different" after a week practising with "some great players" following her second-round Nottingham Open defeat. The 17-year-old beat compatriot Harriet Dart on Tuesday for her first WTA Tour win, having reached the main draw as a lucky loser.

She made an impressive start against world number 27 Marie Bouzkova on Thursday but ultimately faded to a 7-5 6-2 loss. World number 509 Klugman will rise 97 places in the rankings and is already assured of a place in the Wimbledon main draw, having received a wildcard earlier this week.

Tennis.title: Corentin Moutet: Hot-headed Frenchman courts more controversy in HSBC Championship at Queen's Club
Tennis.summary: Corentin Moutet: Hot-headed Frenchman courts more controversy in HSBC Championship at Queen's Club Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Hot-headed Frenchman Corentin Moutet courted more controversy in the HSBC Championship at Queen's Club on Thursday. The 27-year-old had already caused a stir by swearing repeatedly during a live TV interview after his first-round win over Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard.

This time, during his second-round match against Alejandro Davidovich Fokina, Moutet slipped on the court and then angrily slammed his racket on to the surface, a no-no on the manicured lawns of West Kensington.

Tennis.title: Jack Draper - latest news and results
Tennis.summary: Jack Draper - latest news and results Sky Sports
Tennis.body:
Jack Draper, who last month hired Sir Andy Murray to coach him through the grass-court season, ruled himself out of Queen's Club as he continues to recover with a knee problem.

Draper has been back hitting tennis balls at the National Tennis Centre as he prepares for the grass season. The former world No 4 has tumbled outside of the top 100 [world No 113] having already missing Masters 1000 events in Madrid - where he made the final in 2025 - and Rome, due to injury and Queen's.
